info.description: Multi-tenant rights, royalty, and catalog administration platform for music publishers, labels, and managers. Endpoints are grouped by domain (Auth, Catalog, Royalties, Creators, Songs, Contracts, Placements, ...). All non-public endpoints require a Bearer JWT obtained from POST /api/auth/login.
info.title: Cadence Catalog Intelligence API

get.summary: Serve Root

get.description:
Filtered convenience variant of `/organization/{org_id}` — only returns links that are currently active.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `List[LinkResponse]`.

schema.title: Response Get Active Links Api Account Links Active  Org Id  Get

get.summary: List active account links for an organization

get.description:
Returns the org's full set of links regardless of status — pending, active, revoked.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `List[LinkResponse]`.

schema.title: Response Get Links For Organization Api Account Links Organization  Org Id  Get

get.summary: List every account link for an organization

get.description:
Filtered convenience variant of `/organization/{org_id}` — only returns links awaiting target consent.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `List[LinkResponse]`.

schema.title: Response Get Pending Links Api Account Links Pending  Org Id  Get

get.summary: List pending account links for an organization

post.description:
Initiates an AccountLink between the requesting org and a target user/account. The target must consent via `/consent` before the link goes live.

**Body:** `{ org_id, target_email, role?, modules?: string[], message? }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of `org_id`.
**Response:** `LinkResponse` — the pending link header with `status="pending_consent"`.
post.summary: Request to link a target account to the org

put.description:
Patches the granted role and/or module set on an active link. Only the requesting org may modify; target may only revoke.

**Path parameter:** `link_id`.
**Body:** `{ role?, modules?: string[] }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the link's requesting org.
**Response:** `LinkResponse`.
put.summary: Update an account link's role or modules

post.description:
Marks the link as `active` so the requesting org can begin exercising the granted access.

**Path parameter:** `link_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — must be the target user.
**Response:** `LinkResponse` — the link with `status="active"`.
post.summary: Consent to a pending account link

post.description:
Marks the link as `revoked` so the granted access stops immediately. Either side of a link may revoke.

**Path parameter:** `link_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — must be either side of the link.
**Response:** `LinkResponse` — the link with `status="revoked"`.
post.summary: Revoke an active account link

get.description:
Returns every merge request the user has initiated and its current status.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT.
**Response:** `{ requests: [{id, target_email, status, created_at, completed_at}] }`.
get.summary: List the calling user's merge requests

post.description:
Initiates a merge between the calling user's account and a second account they own (e.g. signed up twice). Sends a verification email to the target.

**Body:** `{ target_email: string, reason?: string }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT.
**Response:** `{ request_id, status: "pending_verification", verification_email_sent_to }`.
post.summary: Request a merge of two user accounts

post.description:
Completes a merge using the token from the verification email. Moves the target's data into the calling user, then disables the target login.

**Body:** `{ token: string }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT.
**Response:** `{ status: "completed", merged_user_id }`.
post.summary: Verify and complete an account-merge request

delete.description:
Voids a merge that hasn't been verified yet.

**Path parameter:** `request_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — must be the request initiator.
**Response:** `{ success: true }`.

get.description:
Returns every open action item attached to a specific creator across the modules.

**Path parameter:** `creator_id`.
**Query:** `status`, `priority`, `module`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the creator's org.
**Response:** `{ items: [{id, title, description, module, priority, due_at, status, song_id, created_at}] }`.
get.summary: List action items for a creator

get.description:
Returns the structured list of gaps in a creator's catalog (missing ISRCs, missing splits, songs without contracts, etc.) that drive action-item generation.

**Path parameter:** `creator_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the creator's org.
**Response:** `{ gaps: [{kind, severity, count, examples: [...]}] }`.
get.summary: Get catalog gaps for a creator

post.description:
Like `/generate-org` but uses the broader cross-module rule set (royalty + contract + catalog signals combined) to produce higher-level actions.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ created, skipped, by_module: {...} }`.
post.summary: Generate cross-module action items at the org level

post.description:
Re-runs the gap-detection engine across the entire organization, creating new ActionItem rows where gaps exist and clearing items whose underlying gap is resolved. Idempotent and safe to call repeatedly.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Body:** `{ modules?: string[] }` to limit the engine to a subset of modules.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ created, cleared, scanned, by_module: {...} }`.
post.summary: Regenerate action items for an org

post.description:
Runs the action-suggestion engine for one creator: scans for missing splits, expiring contracts, unmatched royalties, etc., and creates ActionItem rows.

**Path parameter:** `creator_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the creator's org.
**Response:** `{ created, skipped, items: [...] }`.
post.summary: Generate suggested action items for a creator

get.description:
Returns the org's action item queue. Action items are tasks the gap-detection engine and other modules raise (missing splits, expiring contracts, etc.) for users to resolve.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `status` (`open|in_progress|completed|dismissed`), `priority`, `assignee_id`, `due_before`, `due_after`, `module`, `limit`, `offset`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ total, items: [{id, title, description, module, priority, due_at, status, assignee_id, creator_id, song_id, created_at}] }`.
get.summary: List action items in an org

post.description:
Manually creates an action item. Most action items are auto-generated by the gap-detection engine, but ops/admins can also raise items by hand.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Body:** `{ title, description?, module?, priority?: "low"|"normal"|"high", due_at?: datetime, assignee_id?: int, creator_id?: int, song_id?: int }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** the created action item.
post.summary: Create an action item

get.description:
Aggregate counts by module, severity, and assignee for the dashboard tile.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ total, by_module: [...], by_severity: [...], by_assignee: [...] }`.
get.summary: Get the action-items summary for the org

delete.description:
Hard-deletes the action item. Differs from completing it — completed items keep history; deleted ones don't.

**Path parameter:** `action_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the action's org.
**Response:** `{ success: true }`.

put.description:
Patches an action item's editable fields (title, description, due date, priority, assignee).

**Path parameter:** `action_id`.
**Body:** any subset of `{ title, description, priority, due_at, assignee_id, status }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the action's org.
**Response:** the updated action item.

post.description:
Closes an action item, stamping `completed_at` and `completed_by` to the calling user. Idempotent — a second completion is a no-op.

**Path parameter:** `action_id`.
**Body:** `{ resolution_note?: string }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the action's org.
**Response:** the updated action item with `status="completed"`.
post.summary: Mark an action item complete

get.description:
Aggregates AICall log rows by feature and month and returns totals plus recent activity for the super-admin cost dashboard.

**Optional query:** `org_id` (scope to a single tenant), `from`/`to` (ISO dates).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform super-admin only.

**Response:** `{ totals: { calls, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, cost_usd }, current_month: {...}, by_feature: [...], by_month: [...], recent_calls: [...] }`.
get.summary: AI/LLM usage and spend across the platform

post.description:
Internal endpoint used by background workers and other services to log a single AI/LLM call into the AICall table for cost tracking. Not intended for partner integrators.

**Body:** `{ feature, model, provider, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, cost_usd, org_id?, user_id?, metadata? }`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform super-admin only.

**Response:** `{ status: "logged" }`.
post.summary: Record an AI call (internal telemetry endpoint)

post.description:
Asynchronously walks every active source and refetches its historical archive (where supported) so the chart store has deeper history. Long-running.

**Body:** `{ source_ids?: int[], start_date?, end_date? }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform admin.
**Response:** `{ status: "queued", message }`.
post.summary: Kick off a historical chart-data backfill

post.description:
Bypasses the schedule and runs a fetch right now — useful for debugging a source that's failing.

**Path parameter:** `source_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform admin.
**Response:** `{ source_id, status, fetched_entries, error? }`.
post.summary: Trigger an immediate fetch of a chart source

get.description:
Returns every ChartSource the platform polls for chart positions (Billboard, Spotify Charts, Apple Music charts, etc.) along with their fetch status.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform admin.
**Response:** `{ sources: [{id, name, platform, url, status, last_fetched_at, next_fetch_at, error}] }`.
get.summary: List configured chart-data sources

post.description:
Adds a new chart source the fetcher should poll on a schedule. Validates the URL is reachable.

**Body:** `{ name, platform, url, schedule_cron?, country?: string }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform admin.
**Response:** `{ id, name, status: "active" }`.
post.summary: Register a new chart-data source

put.description:
Patches an existing ChartSource (URL, schedule, status). Pass `status="paused"` to stop polling without deleting.

**Path parameter:** `source_id`.
**Body:** any subset of writable fields from create + `status`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform admin.
**Response:** `{ id, status }`.

get.description:
Returns aggregate fetcher metrics for the admin overview tile.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform admin.
**Response:** `{ total_sources, active_sources, by_platform: [...], total_entries, matched_entries, match_rate }`.
get.summary: Get chart-fetcher health statistics

get.description:
Streams a CSV summarising AI, storage, and integration costs per organization for the requested period. One row per (org, cost_category) tuple. Used for monthly invoicing.

**Optional query:** `from`, `to` (ISO dates; default = current month).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform super-admin only.

**Response:** `text/csv` streaming download.
get.summary: Download the platform cost report as CSV

post.description:
Sets the calling super-admin's effective organization context to `org_id` for the remainder of the session, so subsequent calls behave as if the admin were a member of that org. The action is audit-logged.

**Path parameter:** `org_id` — Organization to impersonate.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform super-admin only.

**Response:** `{ message, organization_id, organization_name }`.
post.summary: Switch the super-admin's session into an organization

get.description:
Returns every supported integration (Spotify, Dropbox, Google Drive, OpenAI, Resend, etc.) with whether platform credentials are configured and how many tenants have connected an account.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform super-admin only.

**Response:** `{ total, connected, integrations: [{id, name, category, configured, tenants_connected, last_synced_at}] }`.
get.summary: List third-party integrations and connection status

post.description:
Performs a no-op call against the third-party provider using the platform-level credentials (e.g. fetch profile, list buckets) and returns whether it succeeded. Does not exercise any tenant's OAuth tokens.

**Path parameter:** `integration_id` — integration slug from `GET /integrations`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform super-admin only.

**Response:** `{ success: bool, message }`.
post.summary: Smoke-test an integration's platform credentials

get.description:
Returns every Lead record (waitlist, demo request, investor inquiry, intern application) for the platform admin lead-management view.

**Query:** `kind` (`waitlist|demo|investor|intern`), `status`, `q` (substring on name/email/company), `limit`, `offset`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform super-admin only.
**Response:** `{ total, leads: [{id, kind, name, email, company, status, source, created_at}] }`.
get.summary: List leads in the admin console

get.description:
Streams the resume file an applicant uploaded as part of an intern application.

**Path parameter:** `lead_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform super-admin only.
**Response:** the resume file with the appropriate `Content-Type`.
get.summary: Download a lead's submitted resume

get.description:
Returns every AccountMergeRequest in the system regardless of user, with the calling user's review actions enabled.

**Query:** `status` (`pending|verified|approved|rejected|cancelled`), `q` (email substring), `limit`, `offset`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform super-admin only.
**Response:** `{ total, requests: [{id, source_user_id, target_user_id, target_email, status, created_at, completed_at, reason}] }`.
get.summary: List all account-merge requests for staff review

put.description:
Marks a verified merge request as `approved` and runs the actual data merge — the source user inherits the target's organizations, creators, and history.

**Path parameter:** `request_id`.
**Body:** `{ note?: string }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform super-admin only.
**Response:** `{ status: "approved", message }`.

Marks the request as `rejected` and notifies the requester. No data is moved.

**Path parameter:** `request_id`.
**Body:** `{ reason?: string }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform super-admin only.
**Response:** `{ status: "rejected", message }`.

get.description:
Cross-tenant directory of every Organization row including inactive ones. Used by the super-admin console to power tenant switching, billing, and impersonation.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform super-admin only.

**Response:** `List[OrganizationResponse]` ordered by `name`.

schema.title: Response List All Organizations Api Admin Organizations Get

get.summary: List every organization (super-admin)

post.description:
Creates an Organization row with the requested account type and branding defaults. Does **not** create members — use `POST /organizations/{org_id}/members` afterwards (or trigger an invite from `tenant-admin/org/{org_id}/invite`).

**Body (`OrganizationCreate`):** `name`, `display_name?`, `account_type` (`ARTIST` / `LABEL` / `PUBLISHER` / etc.), `primary_color?`, `secondary_color?`, `tagline?`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform super-admin only.

**Response:** the created `OrganizationResponse`.
post.summary: Provision a new organization (super-admin)

delete.description:
Permanently removes the Organization plus every row scoped to it — Creators, Songs, Releases, Contracts, Royalties, etc. Irreversible; only use after a customer offboarding.

**Path parameter:** `org_id` — Organization ID.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform super-admin only.

**Response:** `{ message: "Organization deleted" }`.
delete.summary: Hard-delete an organization and its data (super-admin)

put.description:
Patches any field on the target Organization. Tenant admins should use `PUT /api/tenant-admin/branding` for the user-facing subset of these fields.

**Path parameter:** `org_id` — Organization ID.
**Body (`OrganizationUpdate`):** any subset of the writable Organization fields (name, display_name, account_type, branding, status, etc).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform super-admin only.

**Response:** the updated `OrganizationResponse`.

post.description:
Creates an OrganizationMember row binding an existing User to the target Organization with the requested role. Use the tenant-admin invite endpoint for the standard email-driven onboarding flow.

**Path parameter:** `org_id` — Organization ID.
**Body:** `{ user_id: int, role?: "OWNER"|"ADMIN"|"MEMBER" }`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform super-admin only.

**Response:** `{ message: "Member added" }`.
post.summary: Attach an existing user to an organization (super-admin)

delete.description:
Deletes the OrganizationMember row binding the user to the org. The User account itself is preserved.

**Path parameters:** `org_id` — Organization ID; `user_id` — User row id.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform super-admin only.

**Response:** `{ message: "Member removed" }`.
delete.summary: Detach a user from an organization (super-admin)

post.description:
Companion to `/run-reminders` focused on user-assigned action items: emits in-app notifications for items due soon and overdue. Run nightly by the scheduler.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform super-admin only.

**Response:** `{ message, upcoming_reminders, overdue_notifications, details }`.
post.summary: Manually trigger the action-item reminder sweep

post.description:
Runs the same reminder/notification job that the platform scheduler executes once per day: finds upcoming releases, expiring contracts, registration deadlines, etc., and writes Notification rows for the relevant users.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform super-admin only.

**Response:** `{ message, notifications_created }`.
post.summary: Manually trigger the daily reminder sweep

post.description:
Renders and emails (via Resend) the weekly catalog/royalty digest to every member of the org with `digest_opt_in = true`. Useful for previewing digest contents on demand.

**Path parameter:** `org_id` — Organization ID.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform super-admin only.

**Response:** `{ message }`.
post.summary: Send the weekly digest email to an organization

get.description:
Cheap aggregate counts used by the super-admin home page. Numbers are computed live (no cache).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform super-admin only.

**Response:** `{ total_users, active_users, total_organizations, total_creators, total_songs }`.
get.summary: Platform-wide totals for the super-admin dashboard

get.description:
Returns every SupportTicket across the platform, plus aggregate counts (`open_count`, `in_progress_count`, `resolved_count`, `closed_count`) for the dashboard header.

**Optional query:** `status`, `category`, `org_id`, `q` (substring match on subject/description), `limit`, `offset`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform super-admin only.

**Response:** `{ tickets: [{id, subject, description, category, status, priority, org_id, user_id, created_at, closed_at, admin_notes, attachments}], open_count, in_progress_count, resolved_count, closed_count, total }`.
get.summary: List support tickets with status counts

put.description:
Overwrites the `admin_notes` field on a SupportTicket with the supplied text. These notes are visible to platform staff only — the requesting tenant cannot see them.

**Path parameter:** `ticket_id` — SupportTicket id.
**Body:** `{ admin_notes: string }`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform super-admin only.

**Response:** `{ ticket_id, status: "updated" }`.
put.summary: Update the admin-only notes on a support ticket

put.description:
Transitions a SupportTicket between `OPEN`, `IN_PROGRESS`, `RESOLVED`, and `CLOSED`. Setting `RESOLVED` or `CLOSED` stamps `closed_at`; reopening clears it.

**Path parameter:** `ticket_id` — SupportTicket id.
**Body:** `{ status: "OPEN"|"IN_PROGRESS"|"RESOLVED"|"CLOSED" }`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform super-admin only.

**Response:** `{ ticket_id, status: "updated", new_status }`.
put.summary: Move a support ticket through its workflow

post.description:
Forces a recompute of the cached `health_score` field on every Song in the platform. Use after model/scoring changes; the scheduler also runs this nightly.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform super-admin only.

**Response:** `{ message, songs_synced }`.
post.summary: Recompute song health scores across every organization

post.description:
Same as `/sync-health-scores` but scoped to a single org — much faster when you only need to refresh after a tenant-specific import.

**Path parameter:** `org_id` — Organization ID.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform super-admin only.

**Response:** `{ message, songs_synced }`.
post.summary: Recompute song health scores for one organization

get.description:
Cross-tenant user listing for the platform super-admin console. Includes inactive accounts and platform staff.

**Optional query:** `q` (substring match on username/email), `limit`, `offset`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform super-admin only.

**Response:** `List[UserResponse]` ordered by `created_at` desc.

schema.title: Response List All Users Api Admin Users Get

get.summary: List every user across the platform

post.description:
Provisions a new User row directly, bypassing the normal signup and invite flows. Optionally attaches the user to an existing organization with a specified role.

**Body (`UserCreate`):** `username`, `email`, `password`, `role` (free-form), `is_active`, `is_admin`, `is_super_admin`, `organization_id?`, `organization_role?` (`OWNER`/`ADMIN`/`MEMBER`).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform super-admin only.

**Response:** the created `UserResponse`.
post.summary: Create a user account (super-admin)

delete.description:
Permanently removes the User row and all OrganizationMember rows attached to it. Audit and authorship rows that reference the user are nulled rather than cascade-deleted. Cannot be used on the calling super-admin (returns 400).

**Path parameter:** `user_id` — User row id.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform super-admin only.

**Response:** `{ message: "User deleted" }`.

get.description:
Returns the full user record across tenants, including admin flags and primary organization membership.

**Path parameter:** `user_id` — User row id.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform super-admin only.

**Response:** `UserResponse`. 404 if not found.
get.summary: Get a single user (super-admin)

put.description:
Patches editable fields on any user, including platform admin flags. To rotate a password, supply `password` in the body — it is hashed server-side.

**Path parameter:** `user_id` — User row id.
**Body (`UserUpdate`):** any subset of `username`, `email`, `password`, `role`, `is_active`, `is_admin`, `is_super_admin`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform super-admin only.

**Response:** the updated `UserResponse`.

get.description:
Returns totals across every org in the system (orgs, users, songs, releases, contracts, statements). Used on the admin dashboard.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform admin only.
**Response:** `{ orgs, users, creators, songs, works, releases, contracts, statements, by_plan: {...} }`.
get.summary: Platform-wide cross-org analytics for staff

get.description:
Returns the cumulative count of songs/works/releases over time, bucketed by month.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `start_date`, `end_date`, `granularity` (`month|quarter`).
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ series: [{period, songs, works, releases}] }`.
get.summary: Catalog growth time series

get.description:
Returns ranked creator metrics (catalog size, earnings, placements) used by the analytics > creators tab.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `start_date`, `end_date`, `metric` (`earnings|songs|placements`), `limit`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ creators: [{creator_id, name, song_count, earnings_cents, placement_count}] }`.
get.summary: Per-creator analytics summary

get.description:
Returns contracts whose `term_end_date` falls inside the lookahead window so the user can renegotiate / renew in time.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `days_ahead` (default 90).
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ contracts: [{id, name, counterparty, term_end_date, days_remaining}] }`.
get.summary: Contracts expiring in the configurable window

get.description:
Renders the named analytics report into the chosen format.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`; `report_type` (`overview|catalog-growth|revenue|creators|placements|rights-coverage|valuation`).
**Query:** `format` (`csv|pdf`), plus the same filters as the report's GET endpoint.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** the rendered file as a download.
get.summary: Export an analytics report as CSV/PDF

get.description:
Returns the count of songs/releases in each health bucket (green/yellow/red) and the most common failure reasons. Used by the catalog-health donut.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ by_status: {green, yellow, red}, top_failures: [{key, label, count}] }`.
get.summary: Distribution of release-readiness health scores

get.description:
Returns the top-of-page KPIs (catalog size, active contracts, earnings YTD, pending action items, etc.) used on the analytics dashboard.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `start_date`, `end_date`, `currency`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ catalog_size, active_contracts, earnings_ytd_cents, pending_actions, creator_count, release_count, ... }`.
get.summary: Get the org-wide analytics overview / KPI tiles

get.description:
Counts of placements by status and revenue per period.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `start_date`, `end_date`, `granularity`, `media_type`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ series: [{period, placed, fee_cents}], by_media_type: [...] }`.
get.summary: Sync placement analytics over time

get.description:
Aggregates royalty allocation amounts over time, optionally grouped by source or holder.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `start_date`, `end_date`, `granularity` (`month|quarter|year`), `group_by` (`source|holder|none`), `currency`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ series: [{key, points: [{period, amount_cents}]}] }`.
get.summary: Revenue analytics time series

get.description:
How much of the catalog has fully-allocated splits vs. partially allocated vs. missing.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ total_works, fully_allocated, partially_allocated, missing, by_pro: [...] }`.
get.summary: Rights/splits coverage analytics

get.description:
Returns the current portfolio valuation summary (DCF + multiples) for the catalog. Lighter weight than the underwriting endpoint.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `discount_rate`, `multiple`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ npv_cents, multiple_value_cents, low_high_band: [low, high], top_contributors: [...] }`.
get.summary: Catalog valuation snapshot

post.description:
Sends a chat turn to the in-app assistant (OpenAI-backed) with the calling user's org context. Returns the assistant reply and any tool calls made in the process.

**Body:** `{ messages: [{role, content}], context?: {org_id?, creator_id?, page?}, stream?: bool }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT.
**Response:** `{ reply, tool_calls?: [...], usage: {prompt_tokens, completion_tokens} }`.
post.summary: Send a message to the AI assistant

post.description:
Bulk variant of `/analyze` — used to backfill analysis after a large import.

**Body:** `{ asset_ids: int[] }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of every asset's org.
**Response:** `{ queued, skipped }`.
post.summary: Queue analysis for many audio assets at once

get.description:
Convenience endpoint for the release page: returns the union of AudioAssets across every Song on the release, grouped by track.

**Path parameter:** `release_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must have access to the release's org.
**Response:** `{ tracks: [{song_id, song_title, assets: [...]}] }`.
get.summary: List audio assets attached to any track on a release

post.description:
Atomic multi-link helper. Each entry binds one storage object to one song with a chosen kind. Used by the "Attach a folder" uploader on the release page.

**Path parameter:** `release_id`.
**Body:** `{ links: [{song_id, storage_object_id, kind}] }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the release's org.
**Response:** `{ created: [...], skipped: [...] }`.
post.summary: Bulk-link several storage objects to several tracks on a release

get.description:
Returns every AudioAsset linked to the song (master, instrumental, stems, demos, alts) in the order they were attached. Use this on the song detail page to render the audio block.

**Path parameter:** `song_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must have access to the song's org.
**Response:** `{ assets: [{id, kind, filename, duration_sec, bitrate, content_type, file_url, waveform_url, analysis_status, uploaded_at}] }`.
get.summary: List the audio assets attached to a song

post.description:
Creates an AudioAsset row pointing at a file already in the org's object store (uploaded via `/api/storage` or imported from a linked Dropbox/Drive). Pass `kind` to label it (master, instrumental, stem, etc.).

**Path parameter:** `song_id`.
**Body:** `{ storage_object_id: int, kind: "master"|"instrumental"|"stem"|"demo"|"alt", label?: string }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the song's org.
**Response:** the new AudioAsset object.
post.summary: Link an existing storage object as an audio asset on a song

get.description:
Returns every AudioTag definition usable across the platform (genre, mood, instrumentation, energy levels). Used to populate the tag picker on assets.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT.
**Response:** `{ tags: [{id, name, category, color}] }`.
get.summary: List the global library of audio tags

delete.description:
Deletes the AudioAsset link. The underlying storage object is preserved so it can be re-linked elsewhere.

**Path parameter:** `asset_id` — AudioAsset id.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the asset's org.
**Response:** `{ success: true }`.
delete.summary: Detach an audio asset from its song

get.description:
Returns the cached analysis output (waveform, BPM, key, LUFS, duration). Returns `status` = `pending` if the worker hasn't completed yet.

**Path parameter:** `asset_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the asset's org.
**Response:** `{ status, duration_sec, bpm, key, lufs, waveform_url, analyzed_at }`.
get.summary: Get the analysis result for an audio asset

post.description:
Queues the asset for the analysis worker which produces a waveform PNG, BPM/key estimate, LUFS reading, and `waveform_url`. Idempotent if analysis is already in progress.

**Path parameter:** `asset_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the asset's org.
**Response:** `{ asset_id, status: "queued"|"running" }`.
post.summary: Trigger waveform/loudness/key analysis on a single audio asset

post.description:
Attaches a tag from the global library to an AudioAsset. Idempotent: re-adding the same tag is a no-op.

**Path parameter:** `asset_id`.
**Body:** `{ tag_id: int }` (or `{ name: string }` to create-on-demand).
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the asset's org.
**Response:** `{ asset_id, tag: {id, name, category} }`.
post.summary: Add a tag to an audio asset

delete.description:
Detaches the tag link. Does not delete the global tag.

**Path parameters:** `asset_id`, `tag_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the asset's org.
**Response:** `{ success: true }`.
delete.summary: Remove a tag from an audio asset

put.description:
Switches an AudioAsset between `master`, `instrumental`, `stem`, `demo`, and `alt`. Used when an upload was tagged incorrectly.

**Path parameter:** `asset_id`.
**Body:** `{ kind: string }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the asset's org.
**Response:** the updated AudioAsset object.
put.summary: Change an audio asset's kind label

get.description:
Returns the org's audit trail (provisioning, deletes, role changes, payments, etc.) with paging. Used by the org settings > audit log page.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `category` (`auth|catalog|royalties|admin|...`), `actor_user_id`, `start_date`, `end_date`, `limit` (default 50), `offset`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be an admin of the org.
**Response:** `{ total, entries: [{id, category, action, actor_user_id, actor_email, target_type, target_id, summary, metadata, created_at}] }`.
get.summary: List org audit log entries

get.description:
Aggregated counts of audit events per category for the dashboard tile.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `start_date`, `end_date`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be an admin of the org.
**Response:** `{ totals: {by_category: {...}, by_actor: [...]}, period: {start, end} }`.
get.summary: Audit log summary

put.description:
Verifies the supplied `current_password` before rotating to `new_password`. Existing sessions are not invalidated by a password change — call `/auth/logout-all` after if you want to force re-login on every device.

**Body:** `{ current_password, new_password }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT.
**Response:** `{ success: true }`. Returns 401 if `current_password` is wrong.

post.description:
Username login is case-insensitive. Returns a Bearer JWT plus the user payload. Use the token in the `Authorization: Bearer ...` header on subsequent calls. Supports OAuth2 password-flow form encoding for the OpenAPI playground.

**Body (form-encoded):** `username, password`.
**Auth:** None.
**Response:** `{ access_token, token_type: "bearer", user: {id, email, full_name, role, organization_id} }`. Returns 401 on bad credentials.
post.summary: Log in (username + password)

post.description:
Creates a new user account. The very first user in a fresh deployment is automatically promoted to admin. Returns a Bearer JWT plus the user payload so the client can sign the user in immediately.

**Body:** `{ email, password, full_name?, organization_name? }`.
**Auth:** None — public sign-up.
**Response:** `{ access_token, token_type: "bearer", user: {id, email, full_name, role, organization_id} }`.
post.summary: Register new user

post.description:
Composite search powered by the brief-builder UI: combines metadata filters (genre, mood, BPM, key), free text, and creator filters into a ranked list of song candidates for a sync brief.

**Body:** `{ org_id, query?, filters: {genres?, moods?, bpm_min?, bpm_max?, key?, creator_ids?, has_audio?, has_one_stop?}, limit?, offset? }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ total, results: [{song_id, title, artist, score, matched_filters: [...]}] }`.
post.summary: Search the catalog with brief-builder filters

get.description:
Type-ahead search across songs, works, releases, creators, contracts, and contacts in a single round-trip — drives the command palette / global search.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `q` (required), `limit` (default 5 per type).
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ songs: [...], works: [...], releases: [...], creators: [...], contracts: [...], contacts: [...] }`.
get.summary: Global cross-entity search for the org

put.description:
Applies the same patch to every supplied song id. Useful for tag/genre/artist re-labels.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Body:** `{ song_ids: int[], patch: {field: value, ...} }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ updated, failed: [{song_id, error}] }`.
put.summary: Bulk-update many songs in one call

post.description:
Adds the supplied credit (writer/producer/etc.) to every song id in the list. Skips songs that already have an identical credit.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Body:** `{ song_ids: int[], credit: {contact_id?, name?, role, share_pct?} }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ added, skipped, failed: [...] }`.
post.summary: Bulk-assign a credit to many songs

get.description:
Renders an Excel report of the catalog (songs, splits, ISRCs, registration status) and streams it as a download.

**Path parameter:** `catalog_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the catalog's org.
**Response:** `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet` download.
get.summary: Generate comprehensive Excel report for a catalog

get.description:
Searches the org's catalog by song title or primary artist. Falls back to a small mock external dataset if no local matches are found (used to demo the search UI on empty orgs).

**Query:** `q` (required), `org_id`, `limit` (default 25).
**Auth:** Bearer JWT.
**Response:** `{ results: [{song_id?, title, artist, isrc?, source: "local"|"external"}] }`.
get.summary: Search for songs by title or artist name

get.description:
Returns the org's songs with optional filtering by catalog, status, or text. Lower-level than `/api/songs`.

**Query:** `org_id`, `catalog_id`, `status`, `q`, `limit`, `offset`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `List[SongResponse]`.

schema.title: Response Get Songs Api Catalog Songs Get

get.summary: List songs with optional filters (legacy catalog list)

get.description:
Returns the song with credits, splits, audio assets, and DSP links inlined. Lower-level than `/api/songs/{id}`.

**Path parameter:** `song_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the song's org.
**Response:** `SongDetailResponse`.
get.summary: Get a song's full detail (legacy catalog endpoint)

get.description:
Returns one summary row per Catalog the user can see (song count, last upload, owner). Used to render the catalog list page.

**Query:** `org_id` (defaults to the caller's current org).
**Auth:** Bearer JWT.
**Response:** `List[CatalogSummaryResponse]` — `[{id, name, song_count, owner, last_upload_at, status}]`.

schema.title: Response Get Catalog Summary Api Catalog Summary Get

get.summary: Get summary for all catalogs

get.description:
Generates and serves the official Cadence Schedule A upload template (Excel) so users have the exact column set the parser expects.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT.
**Response:** `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet` download containing the empty template with header row + sample row.
get.summary: Generate and serve the official Cadence Schedule A upload template

post.description:
Uploads a Schedule A template (PDF or Excel), runs the AI parser, and creates a new Catalog with one Song per parsed row. The unparsed file is also archived for audit.

**Body (multipart/form-data):** `file` — the Schedule A; `org_id`; `name?`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of `org_id`.
**Response:** `{ catalog_id, name, parsed_rows, songs_created, warnings: [...] }`.
post.summary: Upload and parse Schedule A template

get.description:
Returns the creator-facing accounting view: lifetime royalties earned, paid, and balance, plus latest statement period.

**Query:** `org_id`, `currency`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — creator role.
**Response:** `{ lifetime_earned_cents, lifetime_paid_cents, balance_cents, last_statement_period, by_org: [...] }`.
get.summary: Get the creator/client accounting summary

get.description:
Returns songs, works, and releases the calling creator owns or has been granted access to via sharing.

**Query:** `org_id`, `q`, `limit`, `offset`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — creator role.
**Response:** `{ songs: [...], works: [...], releases: [...] }`.
get.summary: Get the catalog visible to the current creator/client

get.description:
For org users: returns every creator profile the org manages or has shared access to.

**Query:** `q`, `org_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT.
**Response:** `{ clients: [{id, display_name, email, linked_creator_id, last_login_at}] }`.
get.summary: List creator/client profiles visible to the user

get.description:
Returns contracts where the creator is a counterparty.

**Query:** `org_id`, `status`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — creator role.
**Response:** `{ contracts: [{id, name, type, status, effective_date, term_end_date}] }`.
get.summary: Get contracts visible to the current creator/client

post.description:
Issues a managed-access invite that lets the target user log in to the creator's portal in a delegated capacity.

**Body:** `{ email, role, modules: string[] }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — creator role.
**Response:** the created link.
post.summary: Grant another user managed access to the creator's portal

get.description:
Returns the access grants the creator has issued to other people (managers, accountants) to view their portal.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — creator role.
**Response:** `{ links: [{id, target_email, role, modules, accepted_at, created_at}] }`.
get.summary: Get the managed-access links for the current creator

get.description:
Returns the calling user's CreatorProfile (when they're acting as a creator/client logged in to the portal): contact info, linked Creator id, theming, and which orgs they're linked to.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — creator role.
**Response:** `{ id, display_name, email, linked_creator_id, linked_orgs: [...], theme }`.
get.summary: Get the current creator/client profile

get.description:
Returns the sync placements where the creator's songs appear.

**Query:** `org_id`, `status`, `start_date`, `end_date`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — creator role.
**Response:** `{ placements: [{id, title, project_name, media_type, status, song_id, song_title, fee_cents, placed_at}] }`.
get.summary: Get sync placements visible to the current creator/client

put.description:
Patches the calling creator's portal-only profile fields (display name, contact email, theme).

**Body:** `{ display_name?, email?, theme? }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — creator role.
**Response:** the updated profile.
put.summary: Update the current creator's portal profile

put.description:
Disables the link so the delegate can no longer use it.

**Path parameter:** `link_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — creator role.
**Response:** `{ success: true }`.

get.description:
Lists creative-directory contacts the org has shared with the calling creator via `/api/creative-directory/.../share-to-client`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — creator role.
**Response:** `{ contacts: [{id, name, role, company, email, phone, photo_url, from_org_id, from_org_name}] }`.
get.summary: Get the directory contacts shared with the current creator

post.description:
Marks the share as `accepted` so the receiver can begin using the granted access.

**Path parameter:** `share_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — must be the receiver.
**Response:** `{ success: true, share: {...} }`.
post.summary: Accept a pending share

get.description:
Returns every ClientShare addressed to the calling user regardless of status (pending invites + already accepted/rejected).

**Query:** `status`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT.
**Response:** `{ shares: [{id, entity_type, entity_id, from_user_id, from_org_name, role, modules, status, created_at}] }`.
get.summary: List shares offered to the current user

get.description:
Convenience filter on `/received` returning only `status = accepted` rows. This is what populates the user's "shared with me" workspace.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT.
**Response:** `{ shares: [...] }`.
get.summary: List actively-accepted shares for the current user

post.description:
Marks the share as `rejected`. Sender is notified.

**Path parameter:** `share_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — must be the receiver.
**Response:** `{ success: true }`.
post.summary: Reject a pending share

post.description:
Marks the share as `revoked` so the receiver loses access. Different from delete — keeps audit history.

**Path parameter:** `share_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — must be the sender.
**Response:** `{ success: true }`.
post.summary: Revoke a share you previously sent

get.description:
Returns every ClientShare the calling user originated, with the current acceptance status.

**Query:** `status` (`pending|accepted|rejected|revoked`), `entity_type`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT.
**Response:** `{ shares: [{id, entity_type, entity_id, target_user_id, target_email, role, modules, status, created_at}] }`.
get.summary: List shares the current user has sent out

post.description:
Creates a ClientShare row offering one of the org's entities (creator, contract, etc.) to a target account with a chosen role + module set. The target sees it under `/received` until they accept.

**Body:** `{ entity_type: string, entity_id: int, target_user_id: int, role: "viewer"|"editor"|"manager", modules: string[] }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT.
**Response:** the created ClientShare object.
post.summary: Share an entity with another account

get.description:
Aggregates over the user's accepted shares to return the distinct creator set they can see across all sharing orgs.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT.
**Response:** `{ creators: [{id, display_name, from_org_id, from_org_name, role, modules}] }`.
get.summary: List clients (creators) the user has access to via shares

get.description:
Aggregates over the user's accepted shares to return the distinct song set they can see.

**Query:** `creator_id` (filter to one shared creator), `q`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT.
**Response:** `{ songs: [{id, title, artist, creator_id, from_org_id, role}] }`.
get.summary: List songs the user has access to via shares

put.description:
Replaces the share's module list (catalog, royalties, contracts, etc.). Only the listed modules are visible to the receiver.

**Path parameter:** `share_id`.
**Body:** `{ modules: string[] }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — must be the sender.
**Response:** `{ share: {...} }`.
put.summary: Update which modules a share grants access to

put.description:
Upgrades/downgrades the receiver's role between `viewer`, `editor`, and `manager`.

**Path parameter:** `share_id`.
**Body:** `{ role: string }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — must be the sender.
**Response:** `{ share: {...} }`.

get.description:
Streams the original uploaded contract file as an attachment download.

**Path parameter:** `contract_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the contract's org.
**Response:** the file with the appropriate `Content-Type` header.
get.summary: Download contract file

get.description:
Returns every Contract linked to the song, newest first.

**Path parameter:** `song_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the song's org.
**Response:** `{ contracts: [{id, name, type, status, effective_date, term_end_date, counterparty, uploaded_at}] }`.

schema.title: Response Get Contracts For Song Api Contracts Song  Song Id  Get

get.summary: List contracts for a song

post.description:
Uploads a PDF/DOCX, persists the binary in object storage, and runs the AI parser to extract terms (parties, dates, royalty splits, advances). The Contract row is linked to the supplied song.

**Path parameter:** `song_id`.
**Body (multipart/form-data):** `file` — the contract document; `name?`; `type?`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the song's org.
**Response:** `{ contract_id, name, type, status, extracted: {parties, effective_date, term_end_date, splits, advance_cents}, warnings: [...] }`.
post.summary: Upload a contract document for a song

delete.description:
Removes a contract record and its stored file. Cascades to ContractDocument rows.

**Path parameter:** `contract_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the contract's org.
**Response:** `{ success: true }`.

get.description:
Returns every CreativeContact in the organization — the directory of writers, producers, A&Rs, attorneys, and other industry contacts curated by the org. Supports text search and tag filtering for the directory list view.

**Path parameter:** `org_id` — Organization ID.
**Optional query:** `q` (substring on name/email/company), `tag` (single tag slug), `linked_creator_id`, `limit`, `offset`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** `{ total, contacts: [{id, name, role, company, email, phone, tags, photo_url, pro_society, ipi, linked_creator_id, shared_to_client, updated_at}] }`.
get.summary: List the org's creative-directory contacts

post.description:
Adds a new contact to the org's directory. Use the `from-creator` endpoint instead when mirroring a Creator that already exists in the roster, and `sync-creators` to bulk-import.

**Path parameter:** `org_id` — Organization ID.
**Body (`CreativeContactCreate`):** `name` (required), `role`, `company`, `email`, `phone`, `bio`, `socials`, `pro_society`, `ipi`, `tags`, `notes`, `linked_creator_id`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** the freshly created contact object.
post.summary: Create a creative-directory contact

post.description:
Sends a single combined email containing several contact cards to the supplied addresses. Each recipient gets one email with every selected contact rendered inline.

**Path parameter:** `org_id` — Organization ID.
**Body:** `{ contact_ids: int[], emails: string[], subject?, message? }`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** `{ success: true, message }`.
post.summary: Email a batch of directory contacts to recipients

get.description:
Audit/lookup endpoint: returns every active (creator, contact) share pair in the org.

**Path parameter:** `org_id` — Organization ID.
**Optional query:** `creator_id` (filter to one creator), `contact_id` (filter to one contact).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** `{ total, shares: [{creator_id, creator_name, contact_id, contact_name, shared_at}] }`.
get.summary: List which contacts are shared with which creators

post.description:
Creates a CreativeContact whose fields are pre-filled from an existing Creator in the roster (name, photo, contact info, PRO/IPI), and sets `linked_creator_id` so future edits to either side can be kept in sync.

**Path parameters:** `org_id` — Organization ID; `creator_id` — Creator to mirror.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** the created CreativeContact object.
post.summary: Mirror a Creator into the creative directory

post.description:
Creates a tokenised, optionally expiring URL that anyone can use (no auth) to view a curated subset of the org's directory in a read-only public page.

**Path parameter:** `org_id` — Organization ID.
**Body:** `{ contact_ids: int[], expires_in_days?: int }`. `expires_in_days` defaults to 30; pass `null` for no expiry.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** `{ success: true, token, contact_count, expires_at }`. Public URL is `/share/directory/<token>`.
post.summary: Mint a public share link for a set of contacts

post.description:
Bulk variant of `/share-to-client`: grants every supplied creator user persistent in-app visibility into every supplied contact. Idempotent per (creator, contact) pair.

**Path parameter:** `org_id` — Organization ID.
**Body:** `{ contact_ids: int[], client_user_ids: int[] }`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** `{ success: true, shared_count }`.
post.summary: Share contacts with multiple client (creator) accounts at once

post.description:
Grants a creator user persistent in-app visibility into a set of the org's directory contacts — the contacts will appear in their creator portal under "Shared with me." Idempotent per (creator, contact) pair.

**Path parameter:** `org_id` — Organization ID.
**Body:** `{ creator_id: int, contact_ids: int[] }`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** `{ success: true, message, created_count }`.
post.summary: Share contacts with a single client (creator) account

get.description:
Walks every Creator in the org and ensures a corresponding CreativeContact exists with `linked_creator_id` set. New contacts are created, existing linked contacts have their core fields refreshed from the Creator. Idempotent.

**Path parameter:** `org_id` — Organization ID.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** `{ message, created_count, created_names: [...], updated_count, updated_names: [...] }`.
get.summary: Bulk-sync the creative directory from the Creator roster

delete.description:
Inverse of `/share-to-client`. Removes the share rows so the supplied creator no longer sees the listed contacts in their portal.

**Path parameter:** `org_id` — Organization ID.
**Body:** `{ creator_id: int, contact_ids: int[] }`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** `{ success: true, message, deleted_count }`.
delete.summary: Revoke client access to one or more shared contacts

delete.description:
Hard-deletes the CreativeContact and any associated client share rows. The linked Creator (if any) is preserved.

**Path parameter:** `contact_id` — CreativeContact id.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the contact's organization.

**Response:** `{ message: "Contact deleted" }`.

get.description:
Returns the full CreativeContact record including bio, social handles, PRO/IPI metadata, tags, share state, and the optional `linked_creator_id` if the contact mirrors a Creator in the org's roster.

**Path parameter:** `contact_id` — CreativeContact id.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the contact's organization.

**Response:** the full contact object (see list endpoint for fields, plus `bio`, `socials`, `notes`, `created_at`).
get.summary: Get a single creative-directory contact

put.description:
Patches editable fields on a CreativeContact. Use the dedicated image endpoints to change `photo_url`.

**Path parameter:** `contact_id` — CreativeContact id.
**Body (`CreativeContactUpdate`):** any subset of the writable fields from the create endpoint. Unspecified fields are untouched.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the contact's organization.

**Response:** the updated contact object.

delete.description:
Removes the contact's photo from object storage and clears `photo_url`.

**Path parameter:** `contact_id` — CreativeContact id.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the contact's organization.

**Response:** `{ message: "Image deleted" }`.

get.description:
Serves the binary image data behind the contact's `photo_url`. Used by the directory UI when the photo is stored privately and must be proxied with auth instead of fetched directly.

**Path parameter:** `contact_id` — CreativeContact id.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the contact's organization.

**Response:** the image bytes with the appropriate `Content-Type`.
get.summary: Stream a contact's profile image

post.description:
Multipart upload of a new profile photo. The file is persisted to the configured object store and `photo_url` is updated to the resulting URL. Replaces any existing photo.

**Path parameter:** `contact_id` — CreativeContact id.
**Body (multipart/form-data):** `file` — the image (PNG/JPEG).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the contact's organization.

**Response:** `{ photo_url }`.
post.summary: Upload or replace a contact's profile image

get.description:
Renders the contact's profile (photo, role, contact info, PRO details, bio) into a one-page PDF that follows the org's branding and streams it as an attachment.

**Path parameter:** `contact_id` — CreativeContact id.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the contact's organization.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ works: [{id, title, writers}] }`.
get.summary: Type-ahead search for works to attach to a placement

get.description:
Returns counts and totals across the placement pipeline for the dashboard tiles.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `start_date`, `end_date`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ total, by_status: {...}, total_fees_cents, average_fee_cents }`.
get.summary: Get sync placement KPI summary

get.description:
Aggregated, branded sync report for the organization. Supports CSV / PDF export downstream.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `start_date`, `end_date`, `format` (`json|csv|pdf`, default `json`).
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** when `format=json`: `{ summary, placements: [...] }`; when `csv|pdf`: file download.
get.summary: Sync placement report

delete.description:
Permanently removes a placement record.

**Path parameter:** `placement_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the placement's org.
**Response:** `{ success: true }`.

get.description:
Returns full placement data including financials, project metadata, and linked song(s).

**Path parameter:** `placement_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the placement's org.
**Response:** `{ id, title, project_name, media_type, license_type, status, song_id, song_title, fee_cents, currency, term_months, territory, client_name, contact_email, notes, placed_at, history: [...] }`.
get.summary: Get placement detail

put.description:
Patches placement fields (project, status, fees, dates, contacts).

**Path parameter:** `placement_id`.
**Body:** any subset of writable placement fields.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the placement's org.
**Response:** the updated placement.

post.description:
Moves a placement to the next pipeline status with validation. Emits the appropriate notifications and audit row.

**Path parameter:** `placement_id`.
**Body:** `{ to_status: string, note?: string }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the placement's org.
**Response:** `{ id, status, transitioned_at, transitioned_by }`.
post.summary: Transition placement status

get.description:
Resolves a share token to the creator's public credits view. Returns 404/410 if the token is invalid or expired.

**Path parameter:** `share_token`.
**Auth:** None — public.
**Response:** `{ creator_name, organization_name, credits: [...], expires_at }`.
get.summary: Public read-only credits page for a creator

post.description:
Public form: captures contact + company info, notifies sales via email, and stores a Lead row.

**Body:** `{ name, email, company?, role?, message? }`.
**Auth:** None — public.
**Response:** `{ success: true }`.
post.summary: Submit a sales demo request

post.description:
Public form for the careers page — captures applicant details and resume URL.

**Body:** `{ name, email, school?, role_interest?, resume_url?, message? }`.
**Auth:** None — public.
**Response:** `{ success: true }`.
post.summary: Submit an intern application

post.description:
Public form for catalog/investor inquiries — captures contact + interest and notifies the team.

**Body:** `{ name, email, firm?, check_size?, message? }`.
**Auth:** None — public.
**Response:** `{ success: true }`.
post.summary: Submit an investor inquiry

get.description:
Public, unauthenticated counterpart to `/share-link`. Resolves a share token to the curated subset of CreativeContacts the org exposed and returns them in a read-only payload, or 404/410 if the token is invalid or expired.

**Path parameter:** `token` — the opaque share token issued by `/org/{org_id}/share-link`.

**Auth:** None — public endpoint.

**Response:** `{ organization_name, expires_at, contacts: [{name, role, company, email, phone, photo_url, bio, socials, pro_society, ipi}] }`.
get.summary: Public read-only view of contacts behind a share token

post.description:
Public, unauthenticated form-submission endpoint that captures an email + role for the marketing waitlist and triggers the welcome email.

**Body:** `{ email, role?, source?, referrer? }`.
**Auth:** None — public.
**Response:** `{ success: true }`. Idempotent on email.
post.summary: Join the public waitlist (marketing site)

post.description:
Internal/admin endpoint to dispatch a push to all of a user's registered subscriptions.

**Body:** `{ user_id, title, body, url?, data? }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — staff/admin.
**Response:** `{ delivered, failed }`.
post.summary: Send a web-push notification to a target user

post.description:
Stores a PushSubscription for the calling user keyed by endpoint. Idempotent on endpoint.

**Body:** `{ endpoint, keys: {p256dh, auth}, user_agent? }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT.
**Response:** `{ subscription_id }`.
post.summary: Register a browser web-push subscription

post.description:
Pushes a sample notification to the calling user's subscriptions for debugging the client-side handler.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT.
**Response:** `{ delivered, failed }`.
post.summary: Send a test push to the current user

post.description:
Deletes the PushSubscription matching `endpoint`.

**Body:** `{ endpoint }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT.
**Response:** `{ success: true }`.
post.summary: Remove a previously-registered web-push subscription

get.description:
Returns the platform's VAPID public key the browser must use when calling `pushManager.subscribe()`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT.
**Response:** `{ public_key }`.
get.summary: Get the VAPID public key for browser web-push subscription

get.description:
Lightweight name/id list of every creator with at least one registerable work or song. Drives the report's creator filter.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ creators: [{id, display_name, work_count, song_count}] }`.
get.summary: List creators eligible to filter the registration report by

get.description:
Streams the report (with current filters applied) as a CSV.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** same filters as `/works` or `/songs`, plus `entity=works|songs`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `text/csv` download.
get.summary: Export the entire registration report as CSV

get.description:
Renders the report (with current filters applied) into a branded PDF.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** same filters as `/works` or `/songs`, plus `entity=works|songs`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `application/pdf` download.
get.summary: Export the entire registration report as PDF

post.description:
Renders only the rows whose ids you specify into a branded PDF (useful when you only care about a few outstanding items). For the unfiltered/full export use the GET variant.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Body:** `{ entity: "work"|"song", ids: int[], pros?: string[], notes?: string }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `application/pdf` streaming download.
post.summary: Export a curated subset of the registration report as PDF

patch.description:
Patches a single field on a single registration row (e.g. set the registered date, paste in a returned work number) without leaving the report. Records the edit in the audit trail.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Body:** `{ entity: "work"|"song", entity_id, pro, field, value }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ success: true, updated: {...} }`.
patch.summary: Inline-edit a registration field from inside the report

post.description:
Persists the supplied filter set + result snapshot so it can be re-opened or refreshed later.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Body:** `{ name, entity, filters: {...} }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** the saved report header.
post.summary: Save the current registration report as a named snapshot

get.description:
Returns each named SavedRegistrationReport snapshot (filter set + frozen results) the org has saved.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ reports: [{id, name, entity, created_at, row_count, last_refreshed_at}] }`.
get.summary: List saved registration reports for the org

delete.description:
Hard-deletes the snapshot.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `report_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ success: true }`.

get.description:
Returns the frozen rows captured the last time the snapshot was refreshed plus its filter set.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `report_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ id, name, entity, filters, last_refreshed_at, rows: [...] }`.
get.summary: Open a previously saved registration report

get.description:
Renders the snapshot (NOT live data) into a branded PDF — useful for distributing a frozen point-in-time view.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `report_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `application/pdf` download.
get.summary: Download a saved registration report as PDF

put.description:
Re-applies the saved filter set to live data and updates the stored snapshot rows + `last_refreshed_at`.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `report_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ id, last_refreshed_at, row_count }`.
put.summary: Re-run a saved report against the current data

post.description:
Builds the report PDF and sends it via Resend to the supplied addresses (e.g. forward to your sub-publisher).

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Body:** `{ entity: "work"|"song", recipients: string[], subject?, message?, ids?: int[], pros?: string[] }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ success: true, sent_to: int }`.
post.summary: Email the registration report to one or more recipients

get.description:
One row per Song showing its DSP/PRO registration health and ISRC/ISWC presence. Used to spot tracks missing a registration.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `creator_id`, `status`, `dsp`, `q`, `limit`, `offset`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ total, items: [{song_id, title, artist, isrc, iswc, registrations: [...]}] }`.
get.summary: Get the songs-side registration status report

get.description:
Returns one row per Work showing where it has been registered (PRO, MLC, SoundExchange, etc.), with a status (`registered`, `pending`, `not_started`). The work-centric counterpart of `/songs`.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `creator_id`, `status`, `pro`, `q`, `limit`, `offset`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ total, items: [{work_id, title, creators, registrations: [{pro, status, registered_at, work_number}]}] }`.
get.summary: Get the works-side registration status report

get.description:
Returns the org's releases with filters for status, type, and creator.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `status` (`draft|ready|distributing|released|archived`), `type` (`single|ep|album|compilation`), `creator_id`, `q`, `limit`, `offset`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ total, releases: [{id, name, artist, type, status, release_date, track_count, artwork_url}] }`.
get.summary: List releases in an organization

post.description:
Creates a new release shell. Tracks are added separately via `/tracks`.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Body:** `{ name, artist, type, release_date?, label?, upc? }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** the created release.
post.summary: Create a release

post.description:
Resolves a Spotify album URL/URI/ID into release metadata (title, artist, label, tracklist, artwork) without importing anything. Used to pre-fill the create-release form.

**Body:** `{ spotify_url_or_id: string }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT.
**Response:** `{ name, artist, label, release_date, spotify_id, artwork_url, tracks: [{position, title, isrc, duration_ms}] }`.
post.summary: Look up a release on Spotify by URL or ID

delete.description:
Removes the release and detaches its track links. Underlying songs are kept.

**Path parameter:** `release_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the release's org.
**Response:** `{ success: true }`.

get.description:
Returns the release with ordered tracks, artwork URL, distribution metadata, and readiness state.

**Path parameter:** `release_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the release's org.
**Response:** `{ release: {...}, tracks: [{position, song_id, title, duration, isrc}], distribution: {...}, readiness: {...} }`.
get.summary: Get release detail

put.description:
Patches release fields (title, type, release date, label, UPC, etc.).

**Path parameter:** `release_id`.
**Body:** any subset of writable release fields.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the release's org.
**Response:** the updated release.

delete.description:
Removes the artwork file from storage and clears `artwork_url`.

**Path parameter:** `release_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the release's org.
**Response:** `{ success: true }`.

get.description:
Returns the binary artwork image; used by the UI when artwork is stored privately and proxied through the API.

**Path parameter:** `release_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the release's org.
**Response:** image bytes with `Content-Type` set.
get.summary: Stream the release's cover artwork

post.description:
Multipart upload — persists the file to object storage and updates `artwork_url`. Replaces any existing artwork.

**Path parameter:** `release_id`.
**Body (multipart/form-data):** `file` — image (PNG/JPEG, ideally 3000×3000).
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the release's org.
**Response:** `{ artwork_url }`.
post.summary: Upload or replace a release's cover artwork

get.description:
Streams a CSV with one row per track and core metadata (title, artist, ISRC, splits, duration).

**Path parameter:** `release_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the release's org.
**Response:** `text/csv` download.
get.summary: Export the release tracklist as CSV

get.description:
Returns the full release object plus its tracks, splits, and credits as a single JSON document — useful for backups or external integrations.

**Path parameter:** `release_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the release's org.
**Response:** the JSON bundle as `application/json`.
get.summary: Export the release as a portable JSON bundle

get.description:
Renders the release with artwork, tracklist, credits, and splits into a branded one-sheet PDF.

**Path parameter:** `release_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the release's org.
**Response:** `application/pdf` download.
get.summary: Export the release one-sheet as PDF

get.description:
Returns the per-check pass/fail breakdown the release page renders as the "health" badge — covers metadata completeness, ISRCs, artwork, splits, and contract coverage.

**Path parameter:** `release_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the release's org.
**Response:** `{ overall: "green"|"yellow"|"red", checks: [{key, label, status, severity, detail}] }`.
get.summary: Run health/readiness checks on a release

get.description:
Computes the readiness checklist for distribution: metadata completeness, artwork, splits, ISRCs, etc.

**Path parameter:** `release_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the release's org.
**Response:** `{ overall: "green"|"yellow"|"red", checks: [{key, label, status, severity, detail}] }`.
get.summary: Release distribution readiness

post.description:
Enqueues the release for the configured distributor (e.g. DistroKid/CD Baby/SonoSuite) — runs the readiness checks, packages metadata + assets, and creates a DistributionJob the UI can poll.

**Path parameter:** `release_id`.
**Body:** `{ provider, dsps?: string[], release_date?: date }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the release's org.
**Response:** `{ job_id, provider, status: "queued" }`.
post.summary: Hand a release off to the distribution provider

post.description:
Appends a song to the release as a new ReleaseTrack with a track / disc number.

**Path parameter:** `release_id`.
**Body:** `{ song_id, position?: int, disc_number?: int }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the release's org.
**Response:** `{ track: {position, song_id, disc_number} }`.
post.summary: Add a track to a release

put.description:
Sets the track order on a release in one atomic call by supplying the desired song id sequence.

**Path parameter:** `release_id`.
**Body:** `{ song_ids: int[] }` — must contain exactly the release's current tracks.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the release's org.
**Response:** `{ release_id, tracks: [{song_id, position}] }`.

delete.description:
Detaches a track from the release without deleting the underlying song.

**Path parameters:** `release_id`, `song_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the release's org.
**Response:** `{ success: true }`.
delete.summary: Remove a track from a release

post.description:
Transitions the release between `draft → ready → distributing → released → archived`. Validates the source/target combination.

**Path parameter:** `release_id`.
**Body:** `{ status: string, note?: string }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the release's org.
**Response:** `{ release_id, status, transitioned_at }`.
post.summary: Move a release through its workflow status

get.description:
Generic asset-keyed view: given an organization plus an (`asset_type`, `asset_id`) pair, returns every contract that touches the asset together with the rights splits attached to it. Used by song / release / work detail pages to render a unified Rights tab.

**Path parameter:** `org_id` — Cadence Organization ID.
**Required query:** `asset_type` (`SONG` / `RELEASE` / `WORK`), `asset_id` (target row id).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** `{ asset_type, asset_id, contracts: [{contract_id, title, contract_type, status, splits: [...]}] }`.
get.summary: Look up the rights stack for any asset

get.description:
Returns every contract in the creator's organization where the creator is either the primary `creator_id` on the Contract row or named as a ContractParty. Useful for the creator profile page's Contracts tab.

**Path parameter:** `creator_id` — Cadence Creator ID.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the creator's organization (admins of the org or super-admins always pass).

**Response:** `{ contracts: [...], total: int }`. Each contract entry carries `id`, `title`, `contract_type`, `status`, `reference_number`, `start_date` and `end_date`. Returns `{ contracts: [], total: 0 }` if there are no matches.
get.summary: List a creator's contracts

delete.description:
Removes the file from storage and deletes the ContractDocument row.

**Path parameter:** `document_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the document's contract org.
**Response:** `{ success: true }`.

get.description:
Streams the binary file as an attachment download.

**Path parameter:** `document_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the document's contract org.
**Response:** the file with the appropriate `Content-Type`.
get.summary: Download a contract document

get.description:
Returns the contract registry for the given organization, ordered by most recently created. Backs the org-level Contracts list view.

**Path parameter:** `org_id` — Cadence Organization ID.
**Optional query:** `status` — when provided, narrows the result to contracts whose `status` matches exactly (e.g. `ACTIVE`, `DRAFT`, `EXPIRED`).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** `{ contracts: [...], total: int }`. Each contract uses the same slim shape as `GET /contracts/song/{song_id}`.
get.summary: List an organization's contracts

post.description:
Creates a Contract row, optionally seeded with parties and asset links in the same call. Use the parser endpoint first to draft the payload from an uploaded document.

**Path parameter:** `org_id` — Cadence Organization ID.
**Body (`ContractCreate`):** `title` (required), `contract_type`, `status`, `reference_number`, `start_date`, `end_date`, `notes`, `creator_id` (primary creator), `parties: [{party_role, party_name, creator_id?, contact_email?}]`, `assets: [{asset_type, asset_id}]`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** the full contract record (same shape as `GET /contracts/{contract_id}` with `include_details=true`).
post.summary: Create a contract in an organization

post.description:
Accepts a contract document (PDF / DOCX / TXT) as multipart upload and runs Cadence's contract parser to extract suggested fields the Create-Contract form can pre-fill (title, type, parties, dates, reference number, asset list).

**Body (multipart):** `file` — the document, ≤ 10 MB.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Read-only with respect to the database — nothing is persisted; the parsed payload is returned for the caller to inspect and POST back to `/contracts/org/{org_id}`.

**Response:** the raw parser dict — typically `{ title, contract_type, reference_number, start_date, end_date, parties: [...], assets: [...], confidence: { ... } }`. 400 on unparsable input.
post.summary: Parse a contract upload into structured fields

get.description:
Returns every Contract that has the song linked as a ContractAsset (`asset_type=SONG, asset_id={song_id}`). The result is the same shape the song detail page uses to render the Rights / Splits sidebar.

**Path parameter:** `song_id` — Cadence Song ID.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. The caller must be a member of the song's organization (or, if the song has a primary credit, of that creator's shared scope).

**Response:** `{ contracts: [...] }` with a slim record per contract: `id`, `title`, `contract_type`, `status`, `reference_number`, `start_date`, `end_date`. Returns `{ contracts: [] }` when nothing is linked. 404 if the song doesn't exist.
get.summary: List contracts attached to a song

delete.description:
Hard-deletes the Contract along with its parties, asset links and RightsSplit rows (cascade). Cached publishing/master percentages on linked songs are not recomputed automatically — re-fetch them via the song split endpoint if needed.

**Path parameter:** `contract_id` — Contract row id.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the contract's organization.

**Response:** `{ message: "Contract deleted successfully" }`. 404 if the contract is missing.

get.description:
