Returns the full Contract record with parties, linked assets, rights splits, and document attachments. This is the source-of-truth fetch the Contract Detail page uses.

**Path parameter:** `contract_id` — Contract row id.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the contract's organization.

**Response:** `{ id, title, contract_type, status, reference_number, start_date, end_date, notes, organization_id, creator_id, parties: [...], assets: [{id, asset_type, asset_id, asset_label, splits: [...]}], documents: [...], created_at, updated_at }`. 404 if not found.
get.summary: Get a contract with full details

put.description:
Patches the top-level Contract fields. Parties, assets and splits have their own dedicated endpoints; this call does not touch them.

**Path parameter:** `contract_id` — Contract row id.
**Body (`ContractUpdate`):** any subset of `title`, `contract_type`, `status`, `reference_number`, `start_date`, `end_date`, `notes`, `creator_id`. Unspecified fields are left untouched.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the contract's organization.

**Response:** the updated contract record (same shape as `GET /contracts/{contract_id}`).

post.description:
Creates a ContractAsset row that pins a Song, Release or Work to the contract. Idempotent: a duplicate (`asset_type, asset_id`) pair returns the existing row instead of erroring.

**Path parameter:** `contract_id` — Contract row id.
**Body (`AssetLink`):** `asset_type` (one of `SONG`, `RELEASE`, `WORK`), `asset_id` (target row id in the matching table).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the contract's organization.

**Response:** `{ id, asset_type, asset_id, message }`.
post.summary: Link an asset (song / release / work) to a contract

delete.description:
Deletes a ContractAsset row by its own primary key (the `asset_id` in the path is the ContractAsset.id, **not** the Song / Release / Work id). Cascades to the RightsSplit rows underneath it.

**Path parameters:** `contract_id`, `asset_id` (ContractAsset id).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the contract's organization.

**Response:** `{ message: "Asset unlinked successfully" }`.
delete.summary: Unlink an asset from a contract

post.description:
Lower-level than the song/release split endpoints: writes a RightsSplit directly against an existing ContractAsset, bypassing the auto-created split-sheet logic. Use this when entering splits from a real source contract (publishing deal, recording agreement).

**Path parameters:** `contract_id`, `asset_id` (ContractAsset id).
**Body (`SplitCreate`):** same shape as the song-split endpoint. The 100% per-rights-type cap is still enforced.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the contract's organization.

**Response:** `{ id, rights_holder_id, rights_type, share_percentage, message }`.
post.summary: Add a rights split under a specific contract asset

get.description:
Returns every ContractDocument for the contract.

**Path parameter:** `contract_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the contract's org.
**Response:** `{ documents: [{id, filename, size, content_type, category, uploaded_at, uploaded_by}] }`.
get.summary: List documents attached to a contract

post.description:
Multipart upload of a contract document (PDF, Word, scanned image). Stored in object storage and recorded as a ContractDocument.

**Path parameter:** `contract_id`.
**Body (multipart/form-data):** `file` (the document); `category?` (`signed`, `draft`, `redline`, `attachment`); `notes?`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the contract's org.
**Response:** `{ id, filename, size, content_type, category, uploaded_at }`.
post.summary: Attach a document file to a contract

post.description:
Adds a ContractParty row to an existing contract.

**Path parameter:** `contract_id` — Contract row id.
**Body (`PartyCreate`):** `party_role` (e.g. `WRITER`, `PUBLISHER`, `LICENSOR`, `LICENSEE`), `party_name` (required when no `creator_id`), `creator_id` (optional Creator FK), `contact_email` (optional).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the contract's organization.

**Response:** `{ id, party_role, party_name, creator_id, contact_email, message }`.
post.summary: Attach a party to a contract

delete.description:
Detaches and deletes a single ContractParty row from the contract. Does not affect the underlying Creator or CreativeContact record.

**Path parameters:** `contract_id`, `party_id`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the contract's organization.

**Response:** `{ message: "Party removed successfully" }`. 404 if the party doesn't belong to the contract.
delete.summary: Remove a party from a contract

get.description:
Streams a CSV split sheet for the contract: one row per (asset, rights_holder, rights_type) tuple with `share_percentage`, `role`, IPI/PRO if known, and totals per rights type at the bottom. Filename is `<contract-title>-split-sheet.csv`.

**Path parameter:** `contract_id` — Contract row id.
**Optional query:** `split_type` — `publishing`, `master`, or `both` (default). Filters which rights types are included.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the contract's organization.

**Response:** `text/csv` streaming download.
get.summary: Export a contract's split sheet as CSV

get.description:
Holder-keyed inverse of `GET /asset/{org_id}`. Returns every contract in the org where the creator appears as a `rights_holder_id` on a RightsSplit row, grouped under their parent contracts.

**Path parameters:** `org_id` — Organization ID; `creator_id` — Creator ID acting as rights holder.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** `{ creator_id, creator_name, contracts: [{contract_id, title, contract_type, status, splits: [...]}] }`.
get.summary: List a rights holder's contracts and shares

get.description:
Mirrors `GET /song-splits/{song_id}` but for Release-level (master) splits. Returns the merged split list across linked contracts plus the release's standalone `Release Splits: <title>` SPLIT_SHEET.

**Path parameter:** `release_id` — Cadence Release ID.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must belong to the release's organization.

**Response:** `{ splits: [...] }` with the same per-split fields as the song endpoint.
get.summary: List rights splits for a release

post.description:
Release counterpart of `POST /song-splits/{song_id}`. Adds a single RightsSplit row to the release's standalone SPLIT_SHEET, creating the contract container on first call. Enforces the per-rights-type 100% cap and ensures a CreativeContact exists for the holder.

**Path parameter:** `release_id` — Cadence Release ID.
**Body (`SplitCreate`):** identical to the song endpoint.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must belong to the release's organization.

**Response:** `{ id, rights_holder_name, rights_type, share_percentage, role, ipi, message }`.
post.summary: Add a rights split to a release

get.description:
Returns the merged set of RightsSplit rows attached to the song across every linked Contract plus the song's standalone `Song Splits: <title>` SPLIT_SHEET (the implicit container Cadence creates when a user adds a split with no source contract).

**Path parameter:** `song_id` — Cadence Song ID.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must belong to the song's org or share the primary creator's scope.

**Response:** `{ splits: [...] }`. Each split exposes `id`, `contract_asset_id`, `rights_holder_id`, `rights_holder_name` (de-referenced through the Creator table when possible), `rights_type` (MASTER / PUBLISHING / PERFORMANCE / MECHANICAL / DISTRIBUTION / SYNC / OTHER), `share_percentage` (0–100), `role`, `notes`, `contract_title`, `contract_id`, and `is_standalone=true` for splits that live in the auto-created split sheet.
get.summary: List rights splits for a song

post.description:
Adds a single RightsSplit row to the song's standalone split sheet, creating the SPLIT_SHEET Contract and ContractAsset on first use. Side effects: refreshes the song's cached `publishing_percentage` / `master_percentage`, syncs the matching SongCredit when the split type is PUBLISHING or MASTER, ensures a CreativeContact exists for the holder, and writes a `record_split_change` audit row.

**Path parameter:** `song_id` — Cadence Song ID.

**Body (`SplitCreate`):** one of `rights_holder_id` (Creator FK) or `rights_holder_name` is required; `rights_type` must be one of MASTER / PUBLISHING / PERFORMANCE / MECHANICAL / DISTRIBUTION / SYNC / OTHER; `share_percentage` is 0.01–100; optional `role`, `notes`, `ipi`, `pro`, `contact_id`. The endpoint rejects with 400 if the new total per `rights_type` would exceed 100%.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT, caller must belong to the song's organization.

**Response:** `{ id, rights_holder_name, rights_type, share_percentage, role, ipi, message }`. 404 if song or named rights holder isn't in the org.
post.summary: Add a rights split to a song

delete.description:
Deletes the RightsSplit row, recomputes the song's cached publishing/master percentages, re-syncs the matching SongCredit, and writes a `record_split_change` audit entry capturing the prior share value.

**Path parameter:** `split_id` — RightsSplit row id.
**Optional query:** `notes` — free-text reason captured in the edit-history record.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must belong to the parent contract's organization.

**Response:** `{ message: "Split deleted successfully" }`. 404 if the split or its asset row is missing.

Generic counterpart to `DELETE /song-splits/{split_id}` for splits that don't live in the auto-created song split sheet. Cascade-safe and re-syncs cached song percentages / SongCredits when applicable.

**Path parameter:** `split_id` — RightsSplit row id.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the contract's organization.

**Response:** `{ message: "Split deleted successfully" }`.

put.description:
Patches share/role/notes/holder on a RightsSplit row. If the split belongs to a song's contract asset, the song's cached publishing/master percentages and the matching SongCredit are re-synced. Edit history is recorded.

**Path parameter:** `split_id` — RightsSplit row id.
**Body (`SplitUpdate`):** any subset of `share_percentage`, `role`, `notes`, `rights_holder_id`, `rights_holder_name`, `rights_type`. The 100% per-rights-type cap is rechecked.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the contract's organization.

**Response:** `{ id, rights_type, share_percentage, message }`.

get.description:
Returns the legacy v1 Advance records for the org (see `/api/royalty-processing/.../advances` for the v2 model). Each advance has a principal, recouped amount, and remaining balance against a creator or contract.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `creator_id`, `contract_id`, `recouped` (bool).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ total, advances: [{id, name, principal_cents, recouped_cents, outstanding_cents, currency, creator_id, contract_id, advance_date, recoupable}] }`.
get.summary: List Advances

post.description:
Records a new advance against a creator or contract that future allocations will recoup against.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Body:** `{ name, principal_cents, currency?, creator_id?, contract_id?, advance_date?, recoupable? (default true), notes? }`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** the created advance object.
post.summary: Create Advance

delete.description:
Hard-deletes an advance. Use with care — historical allocations that recouped against it stay numerically intact but lose the back-link.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `advance_id`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ status: "deleted" }`.

patch.description:
Patches editable fields on an advance. Does not retroactively rewrite recouped amounts.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `advance_id`.
**Body:** any subset of writable fields from create.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** the updated advance object.

get.description:
Returns the RightsSplit-driven allocations computed from calculated statements: who owes how much to whom and from which underlying transaction. Drives the allocations explorer and feeds the payments workflow.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `creator_id`, `contract_id`, `song_id`, `statement_id`, `start_date`, `end_date`, `limit` (default 100), `skip`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ total, limit, skip, allocations: [{id, rights_holder_id, rights_holder_name, song_id, song_title, statement_id, contract_id, amount_cents, currency, share_pct, created_at}] }`.
get.summary: List Allocations

post.description:
Runs the royalty engine: matches transactions to assets, applies rights splits and contract terms, and produces creator allocations + recoupment applications.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `statement_id`.
**Body:** `{ recalculate?: bool }` — set true to wipe and recompute.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ statement_id, matched, unmatched, allocations_created, gross_cents, net_to_creators_cents }`.
post.summary: Calculate royalties for a statement

post.description:
Marks a contract-scoped advance/payment as confirmed — flips the contract's payment state to paid, posts the corresponding RoyaltyPayment, and records the audit trail. Used when an advance check has cleared.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `contract_id`.
**Body:** `{ amount_cents, payment_date, reference?, memo? }`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ contract_id, payment_id, status, amount_cents }`.
post.summary: Confirm Contract Payment

get.description:
Full earnings + payments + advances + fees rollup for a single creator across all statements.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `creator_id`.
**Query:** `period_start`, `period_end`, `currency`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ creator_id, gross_cents, net_cents, paid_cents, balance_cents, advances: [...], by_period: [...], by_source: [...] }`.
get.summary: Per-creator accounting view

get.description:
Per-creator earnings rollup across the org's statements: lifetime gross, paid-out total, outstanding balance, and the amount of revenue still floating in unassigned statements. Drives the creators table on the royalties dashboard.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `start_date`, `end_date`, `currency`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ creators: [{creator_id, name, gross_dollars, paid_dollars, balance_dollars, statement_count}], unassigned_count, unassigned_revenue_dollars }`.
get.summary: Get Creators Royalty Summary

get.description:
Aggregate financial overview: gross revenue, net to creators, fees, advances, and outstanding balances.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `period_start`, `period_end`, `currency`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ gross_cents, net_to_creators_cents, fees_cents, advances_outstanding_cents, paid_cents, balance_cents, by_source: [...] }`.
get.summary: Royalties dashboard summary

get.description:
Aggregates allocation totals grouped by Contract — useful for evaluating whether a deal is recouping or how a publishing agreement is performing.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `start_date`, `end_date`, `currency`, `holder_id`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ earnings: [{contract_id, contract_name, amount_cents, currency, allocation_count}] }`.
get.summary: Earnings By Contract

get.description:
Aggregates allocation totals grouped by rights-holder (creator or company). One row per holder with their share of the org's total earnings over the period.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `start_date`, `end_date`, `currency`, `min_amount_cents`, `limit`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ earnings: [{holder_id, holder_name, holder_type, amount_cents, currency, allocation_count, pct_of_total}] }` sorted by `amount_cents desc`.
get.summary: Earnings By Holder

get.description:
Aggregates allocation totals grouped by Song. Powers the "top tracks" leaderboard.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `start_date`, `end_date`, `currency`, `creator_id`, `contract_id`, `limit` (default 50).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ earnings: [{song_id, song_title, artist, amount_cents, currency, allocation_count}] }`.
get.summary: Earnings By Track

get.description:
Returns the org's RoyaltyFee configurations — admin/processing fees that are subtracted from gross before allocation. Each fee has a scope (org-wide, contract, or creator) and a type (percentage or flat).

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `creator_id`, `contract_id`, `active` (bool).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ total, fees: [{id, name, fee_type, rate_pct, flat_amount_cents, scope, creator_id, contract_id, active, effective_from, effective_to}] }`.
get.summary: List Fees

post.description:
Defines a new processing fee that future statement calculations will deduct.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Body:** `{ name, fee_type: "percentage"|"flat", rate_pct?, flat_amount_cents?, scope: "org"|"contract"|"creator", creator_id?, contract_id?, effective_from?, effective_to? }`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** the created fee object.
post.summary: Create Fee

delete.description:
Hard-deletes a fee. Allocations that previously applied this fee keep their historical numbers; only future calculations are affected.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `fee_id`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ status: "deleted" }`.

patch.description:
Patches an existing fee. Does **not** retroactively recompute already-calculated statements.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `fee_id`.
**Body:** any subset of writable fields from create.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** the updated fee object.

get.description:
Returns recorded payments out to creators with status and amounts.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `creator_id`, `status`, `period_start`, `period_end`, `limit`, `offset`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ total, payments: [{id, creator_id, creator_name, amount_cents, currency, paid_at, method, reference}] }`.
get.summary: List royalty payments

post.description:
Records a cash disbursement to a creator. Updates outstanding balances accordingly and creates an audit entry.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Body:** `{ creator_id, amount_cents, currency, paid_at?: date, method?: string, reference?: string, note?: string }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** the created payment.
post.summary: Record a royalty payment

patch.description:
Patches editable fields on a recorded RoyaltyPayment — most commonly to update `status` (pending → paid → cleared) or fix a wrong `amount_cents` / `payment_date` after entry.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `payment_id`.
**Body:** any subset of `{ status, amount_cents, payment_date, memo, reference }`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ id, status, amount_cents, amount_dollars, payment_date }`.

get.description:
Returns every royalty statement ingested for the organization with paging and status filters.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `status`, `source_type` (`dsp|label|publisher|sync|other`), `period_start`, `period_end`, `limit`, `offset`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ total, statements: [{id, source_name, source_type, period_start, period_end, total_amount_cents, currency, status, uploaded_at}] }`.
get.summary: List royalty statements

post.description:
Bulk-assigns every RoyaltyStatement that currently has no creator attached to a single target creator. Used after import when a batch was uploaded without the creator id set.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Body:** `{ creator_id: int, statement_ids?: int[] }`. When `statement_ids` is omitted, every unassigned statement in the org is reassigned.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ assigned, creator_id, creator_name }`.
post.summary: Assign Unassigned Statements

post.description:
Deletes several statements in one transaction, running the same exhaustive cleanup (advance restore, payment unwind, action-item clearance).

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Body:** `{ statement_ids: int[] }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ deleted: int, transactions: int, allocations: int, advances_restored_cents: int, payments_unwound: int, action_items_cleared: int }`.
post.summary: Bulk Delete Statements

post.description:
Combined non-mutating preview for deleting many statements at once. Returns the same shape as the single-statement preview, summed.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Body:** `{ statement_ids: int[] }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ statements: int, transactions: int, allocations: int, advances_restored_cents: int, payments_unwound: int, action_items_cleared: int }`.
post.summary: Combined non-mutating preview for deleting many statements at once

post.description:
Parses an uploaded statement file in-memory (without persisting anything) and returns the detected source type, suggested column mapping, headers, and the first N rows so the operator can confirm before committing via `/upload`.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Body (multipart/form-data):** `file` — the statement (CSV/XLSX); `preview_rows?` (default 50).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ success, detected_source_type, headers, columns, mapping, preview_rows: [...], row_count }`.
post.summary: Preview Statement

post.description:
Ingests a CSV / XLSX / PDF royalty statement, normalizes columns, and stages it for matching. Supports DSP, label, publisher, and sync statement formats.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Body (multipart/form-data):** `file`; `source_name`; `source_type` (`dsp|label|publisher|sync|other`); `period_start?`; `period_end?`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ statement_id, status, total_rows, total_amount_cents, currency }`.
post.summary: Upload royalty statement

delete.description:
Hard-deletes the statement and all linked transactions, allocations, advance applications, and payment links. Call `/delete-preview` first to confirm the blast radius.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `statement_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** same shape as `/delete-preview` with `success: true` added.

get.description:
Returns the statement header plus a paged view of its transactions.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `statement_id`.
**Query:** `limit` (default 50), `offset`, `unmatched_only?: bool`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ statement: {...}, transactions: {total, items: [{id, title, artist, isrc, units, amount_cents, matched_song_id}]} }`.
get.summary: Get royalty statement detail

get.description:
Returns a non-mutating summary of what `DELETE /statements/.../{statement_id}` would remove (matched transactions, advances to restore, payments to unwind, actions to clear).

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `statement_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ statement_id, transactions: int, allocations: int, advances_restored_cents: int, payments_unwound: int, action_items_cleared: int }`.
get.summary: Return a non-mutating summary of what `DELETE` would remove

post.description:
Manually attaches a single RoyaltyTransaction to a song so it will participate in royalty calculation. Overrides any prior auto-match.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `statement_id`, `transaction_id`.
**Body:** `{ song_id: int }`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ song_id, song_title, detail }`.
post.summary: Manual Match

post.description:
Re-runs the auto-matcher across every unmatched RoyaltyTransaction on a statement (useful after editing the catalog or fixing ISRCs). Confirmed/manual matches are preserved.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `statement_id`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ status, newly_matched, remaining_unmatched }`.
post.summary: Rematch Transactions

get.description:
Returns the per-track RoyaltyTransaction rows that were created from a statement (the v1 transaction model — see royalty-processing for the v2 statement-line model). Supports the transactions table on the statement detail page.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `statement_id`.
**Query:** `q` (search), `matched` (`true|false|null`), `limit` (default 100), `skip`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ total, limit, skip, transactions: [{id, track_title, artist, isrc, period, territory, amount_cents, currency, song_id, song_title, matched_at}] }`.
get.summary: List Transactions

get.description:
Returns every AdvanceV2 record — the principal balances that future royalty allocations will recoup against, including their current recoupment status.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `payee_id`, `contract_id`, `recouped` (bool — only fully/partially recouped), `pool` (recoupment pool name).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ advances: [{id, advance_name, advance_date, principal_amount_cents, recouped_amount_cents, outstanding_cents, currency, recoupment_pool, recoupment_priority, cross_collateralize, payee_id, contract_id, recoupable, start_recouping_on, end_recouping_on}] }`.
get.summary: List all advances (recoupable and non-recoupable) in the org

post.description:
Records an advance that will be recouped from future royalties in the given `recoupment_pool`. Set `cross_collateralize=true` to allow recoupment across all of the payee's pools.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Body (`AdvanceCreateRequest`):** `advance_name`, `principal_amount_cents`, `recoupment_pool`, `recoupment_priority` (default 1), `currency` (default USD), `recoupable` (default true), `cross_collateralize` (default false), `payee_id?`, `contract_id?`, `advance_date?`, `start_recouping_on?`, `end_recouping_on?`, `notes?`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** the created advance object.
post.summary: Create a new advance against a payee or contract

get.description:
Returns the advance plus the per-statement history of how much was recouped against it and when.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `advance_id`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** the full advance object plus `recoupment_history: [{processing_run_id, statement_id, recouped_at, amount_cents}]`.
get.summary: Get a single advance with its recoupment history

put.description:
Patches editable fields on an advance. Changing `principal_amount_cents` does **not** retroactively rewrite ledger entries — re-run `/reprocess` on affected statements if you need that.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `advance_id`.
**Body (`AdvanceUpdateRequest`):** any subset of writable fields from create.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** the updated advance object.

get.description:
Returns the org-wide decay analytics dashboard data: aggregate earnings curves, CAGR, top earners, and projected lifetime value across the catalog.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `start_date`, `end_date`, `currency`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ totals_by_period: [...], cagr, decay_fit: {a, k, r2}, top_songs: [...], projections: [...] }`.
get.summary: Portfolio-level decay/earnings analytics for the org

get.description:
The song-level version of `/analytics/portfolio`: time series, exponential decay fit, CAGR, and projection for a single song based on its historical royalty postings.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `song_id`.
**Query:** `start_date`, `end_date`, `currency`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ song_id, time_series: [{period, amount_cents}], cagr, decay_fit: {a, k, r2}, projection: [...] }`.
get.summary: Per-song decay/earnings analytics

get.description:
Returns a flexible time series of royalty postings, optionally grouped by source, classification, payee, or song. Powers custom analytics widgets.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `start_date`, `end_date`, `granularity` (`month|quarter|year`), `group_by` (`source|classification|payee|song`), `currency`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ series: [{key, points: [{period, amount_cents}]}] }`.
get.summary: Aggregate royalty earnings as a time series

get.description:
Returns the prioritized list of things a royalty operator needs to do — statements waiting to be processed, statements with low-confidence matches, statements that re-need processing after splits changed, etc. Each item includes a deep link.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ items: [{id, kind, severity, statement_id, title, description, created_at, link}] }`.
get.summary: Get the royalty-processing action-item inbox

post.description:
Locks in the song/work/release a statement line should pay out to. If the body specifies `song_id` it overrides whatever the auto-matcher suggested. Sets the line's status to `confirmed` and clears any pending suggestion.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `line_id` — RoyaltyStatementLine id.
**Body (`ConfirmMatchRequest`):** `song_id` (required), `work_id?`, `release_id?`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ success: true }`.
post.summary: Confirm a suggested match (or attach an explicit one) to a line

post.description:
Sets the line's status to `ignored` so it stops appearing in the unmatched queue and is excluded from allocation. Useful for tax adjustments, recoupments already handled elsewhere, or garbage rows from the source file.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `line_id`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ success: true }`.
post.summary: Mark a statement line as intentionally unallocated

post.description:
Clears the suggested song/work/release on a line and returns it to `unmatched` so a human can resolve it. Does **not** mark the line as ignored; use `/ignore` for that.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `line_id`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ success: true }`.
post.summary: Reject the auto-matcher's suggestion on a line

get.description:
Returns the top scoring catalog entries for a line so a human can pick the right one when the auto-matcher was uncertain. Combines ISRC/title/artist matching strategies.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `line_id`.
**Query:** `limit` (default 5).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ line_id, suggestions: [{song_id, work_id, release_id, title, artist, isrc, confidence, reason}] }`.
get.summary: Get ranked match candidates for a single statement line

get.description:
Returns the rolled-up `balance_cents` per payee — i.e. what would be paid out if a batch were cut today, after recoupment. Drives the "Payables" dashboard and the payout-batch builder.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `min_balance_cents` (default 0), `currency`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ payables: [{payee_id, payee_name, payee_type, balance_cents, currency, last_credit_at}] }` sorted by balance desc.
get.summary: List current outstanding amounts owed to each payee

get.description:
Returns every Payee record — the abstraction that owns a royalty ledger balance. A payee is either backed by a Creator in the roster or an external `company` (label, sub-publisher, etc.).

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `payee_type` (`creator|company`), `q` (name search).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ payees: [{id, payee_type, creator_id, company_name, contact_email, balance_cents, currency, created_at}] }`.
get.summary: List the org's payees (creators and external companies)

post.description:
Adds a Payee that future allocations and payouts can post to. When `payee_type="creator"`, `creator_id` must reference a Creator in the org. When `payee_type="company"`, `company_name` must be supplied.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Body (`PayeeCreateRequest`):** `payee_type` (`creator|company`), `creator_id?`, `company_name?`, `contact_email?`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ id, payee_type, creator_id, company_name, contact_email, created_at }`.
post.summary: Create a new payee (creator-backed or external company)

get.description:
Returns every credit (allocation), debit (recoupment), and payout posted to the payee, chronological newest-first, with a running balance. This is the source of truth for what the org owes — or has paid — that payee.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `payee_id`.
**Query:** `limit` (default 100), `offset`, `start_date`, `end_date`, `entry_type` (`credit|debit|payout|advance`).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ total, limit, offset, entries: [{id, posted_at, entry_type, amount_cents, running_balance_cents, currency, statement_id, processing_run_id, payout_item_id, advance_id, memo}] }`.
get.summary: Get a payee's running royalty ledger

get.description:
Returns all PayoutBatch records (drafts, approved, paid) with their total amount and item count. Used to render the payouts page.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `status` (`draft|approved|paid|cancelled`).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ batches: [{id, name, status, currency, total_cents, item_count, created_at, paid_at}] }`.
get.summary: List payout batches for the organization

post.description:
Creates a draft PayoutBatch that line items can be added to via `/items`. Status starts as `draft`; cut over to `approved` and then `paid` via `/status`.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Body (`PayoutBatchCreateRequest`):** `name`, `currency` (default USD).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** the created batch object.
post.summary: Create a new (empty) payout batch

post.description:
Appends a single payee/amount line to a draft PayoutBatch and debits the payee's ledger when the batch is later marked paid. Only allowed while the batch is `draft`.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `batch_id`.
**Body (`PayoutItemCreateRequest`):** `payee_id`, `amount_cents`, `memo?`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** the created PayoutItem `{id, payee_id, amount_cents, memo, created_at}`.
post.summary: Add a payout item to a draft batch

put.description:
Transitions the batch — `draft → approved → paid` (or `cancelled` from any non-paid state). Marking a batch `paid` writes a debit ledger entry for every item against its payee.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `batch_id`.
**Body (`PayoutBatchStatusUpdate`):** `status` (`draft|approved|paid|cancelled`).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** the updated batch object.
put.summary: Move a payout batch through its status lifecycle

post.description:
Accepts a CSV/XLSX statement file (Spotify, Apple, ASCAP, BMI, etc.), auto-detects the source format, suggests column mappings, and creates a RoyaltyStatement with parsed RoyaltyStatementLine rows. Run `/auto-match` next.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Body (multipart/form-data):** `file` (the statement), `source` (PRO/DSP slug, optional — auto-detected when omitted), `period_start`, `period_end` (optional ISO dates), `column_mapping` (optional JSON overrides).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ statement_id, source_detected, lines_created, suggested_mapping, warnings }`.
post.summary: Upload a royalty statement file and parse it into lines

get.description:
Computes — without persisting — the per-payee allocation that `/process` would write to the ledger, by joining each confirmed line to its work's RightsSplits and applying advance recoupment rules. Use this to sanity-check before pulling the trigger.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `statement_id`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ statement_id, is_processed, allocations: [{payee_id, payee_name, gross_cents, recouped_cents, net_cents, currency, lines: [...]}] }`.
get.summary: Preview how a statement's revenue would split across rights-holders

post.description:
Kicks off the auto-matching engine which fingerprints every unmatched line against the org's catalog (ISRC > exact title+artist > fuzzy title) and writes a suggested `song_id`/`work_id` plus a confidence score to each row. Lines already in `confirmed` or `ignored` are skipped. Synchronous.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `statement_id`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ success: true, status, stats: { matched, high_confidence, low_confidence, unmatched } }`.
post.summary: Run the auto-matcher across every line on a statement

post.description:
Walks every line on the statement that has a `match_confidence` at or above the supplied threshold and confirms it in one shot. Designed to clear out the easy wins so reviewers can focus on the long tail.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `statement_id`.
**Body (`BulkConfirmRequest`):** `threshold` (0–100, default 85).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ success: true, confirmed_count }`.
post.summary: Bulk-confirm every high-confidence suggestion on a statement

get.description:
Returns the buckets the reconciliation engine assigned to each line — mechanical, performance, sync, neighboring rights, streaming, etc. — with counts and totals per bucket. Drives the "composition" pie chart on the statement page.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `statement_id`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ classifications: [{bucket, count, amount_cents, currency, pct_of_total}] }`.
get.summary: Break down a statement's lines by revenue classification

get.description:
Streams the rows from `/allocation-preview` as a CSV — one row per payee with their gross, recouped and net amounts.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `statement_id`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `text/csv` download — columns: `payee_id, payee_name, gross_cents, recouped_cents, net_cents, currency`.
get.summary: Download the per-payee allocation preview as CSV

get.description:
Like the allocation export but only for **processed** statements: shows the actual ledger postings written by the processing run plus updated payee balances.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `statement_id`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `text/csv` download — columns: `payee_id, payee_name, posted_amount_cents, balance_cents, currency, processing_run_id`.
get.summary: Download the payables report for a processed statement as CSV

get.description:
Streams a CSV of every line whose status is `unmatched` so an operator can resolve them in a spreadsheet (or hand them off to a contractor).

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `statement_id`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `text/csv` download — columns: `line_id, track_title, artist, isrc, period, territory, amount_cents, currency, suggested_song_id, suggested_title, confidence`.
get.summary: Download the unmatched lines on a statement as CSV

get.description:
Returns the per-track/per-territory rows that were extracted when the statement file was uploaded. This is the workhorse list view for the matching/reconciliation UI.

**Path parameters:** `org_id` — Organization ID; `statement_id` — RoyaltyStatement id.
**Query:** `status` (`unmatched|matched|confirmed|ignored`), `q` (substring on title/artist/ISRC), `min_amount`, `max_amount`, `limit` (default 100), `offset`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ total, limit, offset, lines: [{id, track_title, artist, isrc, period, territory, amount_cents, currency, status, song_id, work_id, release_id, match_confidence}] }`.
get.summary: List the parsed line items on a royalty statement

get.description:
Returns the breakdown the matching dashboard renders along the top of the page: how many lines fall in each status bucket and what they sum to. Used to drive the progress bars and KPIs.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `statement_id`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ total_lines, total_amount, by_status: { unmatched, suggested, confirmed, ignored } }` where each entry is `{ count, amount_cents }`.
get.summary: Get aggregate counts and totals for a statement's lines

get.description:
Reports how much of the statement's value has been matched, by match strategy (ISRC, exact, fuzzy, manual) — useful for spotting brittle data sources.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `statement_id`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ total_amount_cents, matched_amount_cents, unmatched_amount_cents, coverage_pct, by_strategy: [{strategy, count, amount_cents}] }`.
get.summary: Summarize match coverage for a statement

post.description:
Materializes the allocation preview into RoyaltyLedgerEntry rows for every payee, applies recoupment against any open advances in the configured pools, and marks the statement as `processed`. Idempotent on already-processed statements (use `/reprocess` to redo).

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `statement_id`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ success: true, processing_run: {id, started_at, completed_at, lines_processed, entries_written, advance_recoupments_cents} }`.
post.summary: Process a statement: write ledger entries and recoup advances

get.description:
Compares the sum of parsed line amounts against the source file's reported totals (when supplied) to flag parsing/import errors. Used as a sanity check before processing.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `statement_id`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ reported_total_cents, parsed_total_cents, delta_cents, in_balance, by_currency: [...] }`.
get.summary: Run control totals against a statement

post.description:
Voids the prior RoyaltyProcessingRun's ledger entries (keeping an audit trail), then re-runs `/process` from scratch. Use when splits, advances, or matches have changed since the original processing.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `statement_id`.
**Body (`ReprocessRequest`):** `reason` — required free-text audit reason recorded on the new run.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ success: true, processing_run: {...} }`.
post.summary: Reverse and re-run processing for an already-processed statement

get.description:
Returns the audit history of `/process` and `/reprocess` calls against a statement, newest first.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `statement_id`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ runs: [{id, kind, started_at, completed_at, actor_user_id, reason, lines_processed, entries_written, voided_at, voided_by_run_id}] }` ordered by `started_at desc`.
get.summary: List every processing run for a statement

post.description:
Stores the totals printed on the cover page of the original PRO/DSP statement so reconciliation has something to compare the parsed sum against. Use when the parser couldn't auto-extract them.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `statement_id`.
**Body:** `{ reported_total_cents: int, currency: str, totals_by_bucket?: { mechanical?, performance?, ... } }`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the org.

**Response:** `{ success: true }`.
post.summary: Manually record the reported totals from the source file

get.description:
Returns every ScheduleAImport the org has run with summary metrics (rows imported, errors, original filename).

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `status`, `limit`, `offset`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `List[ScheduleAImportSummary]`.

schema.title: Response List Schedule A Imports Api Schedule A Imports  Org Id  Get

get.summary: List Schedule A imports for the organization

get.description:
Streams the original spreadsheet that was uploaded to create this import — useful for forensics when the import produced unexpected data.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `import_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** the file with the appropriate `Content-Type`.
get.summary: Download the original Schedule A import file

post.description:
Re-runs the unified document parser against the saved original file for an existing import — useful when the parser was upgraded after the original run.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `import_id`.
**Body:** `{ options?: {...} }` — optional parser overrides.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ import_id, status, parsed_rows, warnings: [...] }`.
post.summary: Re-run the unified document parser against the saved original file

get.description:
Legacy CSV export endpoint that 308-redirects to `/creator/{creator_id}/csv`. Kept for backward compatibility with older integrations.

**Path parameter:** `creator_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the creator's org.
**Response:** 308 redirect to the new CSV endpoint.
get.summary: Legacy CSV export - redirects to new CSV endpoint

get.description:
Streams the creator's Schedule A as a CSV download.

**Path parameter:** `creator_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the creator's org.
**Response:** `text/csv` download.
get.summary: Export Schedule A as CSV

get.description:
Returns the structured Schedule A rows (titles, splits, contracts) for a creator.

**Path parameter:** `creator_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the creator's org.
**Response:** `{ creator: {...}, rows: [{title, isrc, iswc, writer_splits: [...], contracts: [...]}] }`.
get.summary: Get a creator's Schedule A data

get.description:
Renders a branded PDF Schedule A for the creator with the org logo.

**Path parameter:** `creator_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the creator's org.
**Response:** `application/pdf` download.
get.summary: Export Schedule A as PDF

get.description:
Renders a simplified, share-friendly Schedule A PDF (less internal detail) suitable for sending to a sub-publisher or counterparty.

**Path parameter:** `creator_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the creator's org.
**Response:** `application/pdf` download.
get.summary: Export simplified Schedule A PDF for external sharing

post.description:
Stages a Schedule A PDF/DOCX, runs AI extraction, and returns a parsed preview for review.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Body (multipart/form-data):** `file`; `creator_id?`; `notes?`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ import_id, parsed_rows, creator_id, warnings: [...], preview: [{title, isrc?, splits: [...]}] }`.
post.summary: Upload a Schedule A document

get.description:
Returns every Setting key/value the platform defines (system feature flags, pricing tiers, rate limits, etc.).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform admin only.
**Response:** `List[SettingResponse]`.

schema.title: Response Get Settings Api Settings  Get

get.summary: List platform-wide settings

post.description:
Inserts a new Setting or overwrites an existing one keyed by `key`. Values are JSON.

**Body:** `{ key: string, value: any, description?: string }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — platform admin only.
**Response:** `SettingResponse` — the persisted row.
post.summary: Create or update a platform setting (upsert)

get.description:
Public health probe returning which optional API integrations are configured in this deployment (Spotify, OpenAI, Resend, etc.). Used by the marketing site to indicate live capabilities. **Does not** expose secrets.

**Auth:** None — public.
**Response:** `{ spotify: bool, openai: bool, resend: bool, dropbox: bool, google_drive: bool }`.
get.summary: Public endpoint to check which APIs are configured

post.description:
Creates a DocumentShare row visible to the target account under `/shared-with-me`.

**Body:** `{ entity_type, entity_id, target_user_id, role? }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT.
**Response:** the created share.
post.summary: Share a document with another in-app account

post.description:
Generates a one-time access link to the document and emails it to the supplied recipients via Resend.

**Body:** `{ entity_type, entity_id, recipients: string[], subject?, message? }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT.
**Response:** `{ success: true, sent_to: int }`.
post.summary: Email a sharable document to one or more addresses

get.description:
Outbox view of every DocumentShare the user originated.

**Query:** `entity_type`, `target_user_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT.
**Response:** `{ shares: [{id, entity_type, entity_id, target_email, status, created_at}] }`.
get.summary: List documents the current user has shared out

get.description:
Inbox view — every DocumentShare addressed to the calling user.

**Query:** `entity_type`, `unread` (bool).
**Auth:** Bearer JWT.
**Response:** `{ shares: [{id, entity_type, entity_id, title, from_user_id, from_org_name, created_at, dismissed_at}] }`.
get.summary: List documents shared with the current user

get.description:
Returns users matching the substring search across name + email; used to pick a target for a share.

**Query:** `q` (required), `limit` (default 10).
**Auth:** Bearer JWT.
**Response:** `{ users: [{id, display_name, email, avatar_url}] }`.
get.summary: Type-ahead user search for the share-to-account picker

get.description:
Returns the underlying entity payload behind a share so the receiver can preview it before importing.

**Path parameter:** `share_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — must be the receiver or sender.
**Response:** `{ entity_type, entity_id, payload: {...}, shared_by, shared_at }`.
get.summary: Get full metadata for a shared item

post.description:
Hides the share from `/shared-with-me` without rejecting it. Receiver-only action.

**Path parameter:** `share_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — must be the receiver.
**Response:** `{ success: true }`.
post.summary: Dismiss a share from your inbox

get.description:
Streams the shared entity's exportable representation as a file.

**Path parameter:** `share_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — must be the receiver or sender.
**Response:** the file as a download.
get.summary: Download the shared item as a file (CSV/PDF/etc.)

post.description:
Copies the shared entity (creator, song, work, contract, etc.) into the chosen target org owned by the receiver. Creates a fresh row, not a reference.

**Path parameter:** `share_id`.
**Body:** `{ target_org_id: int, options?: {...} }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — must be the receiver, must own the target org.
**Response:** `{ imported_entity_id, entity_type }`.
post.summary: Import a shared item into the receiver's org

post.description:
Marks the share as revoked so the receiver loses access.

**Path parameter:** `share_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — must be the sender.
**Response:** `{ success: true }`.
post.summary: Revoke a share you previously sent

post.description:
Deletes multiple songs in a single transaction. Only songs the caller's organization owns are removed; foreign songs are silently skipped.

**Body:** `{ song_ids: int[] }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT.
**Response:** `{ deleted, skipped }`.
post.summary: Bulk delete songs

get.description:
Returns every checklist item type the platform defines (with labels, descriptions, due-by rules) — used to render the checklist editor and to validate `PATCH /{song_id}/checklist`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT.
**Response:** `List[ChecklistItemResponse]`.

schema.title: Response Get Checklist Items Api Songs Checklist Items Get

get.summary: List the global catalog of song-checklist items

post.description:
Assigns the supplied songs a shared `group_id` so the catalog treats them as variants (radio edit, instrumental, etc.) of one underlying track.

**Body:** `{ song_ids: int[], primary_song_id?: int }`. The primary song's metadata is treated as canonical.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of every song's org.
**Response:** `{ group_id, song_ids: [...] }`.
post.summary: Group several songs together as variants of one master

get.description:
Returns the org's songs. Supports text search, release-status filter, type filter, and pagination.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `q`, `release_status` (`unreleased|released|archived`), `type`, `creator_id`, `limit`, `offset`, `sort`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ total, songs: [{id, title, artist, isrc, iswc, release_status, health_score, primary_creator_id, updated_at}] }`.

schema.title: Response Get Organization Songs Api Songs Org  Org Id  Get

get.summary: List songs in an organization

post.description:
Adds a new song to the catalog. Creator credits can be supplied at creation; missing creators are auto-created.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Body:** `{ title, artist?, isrc?, iswc?, type?, release_status?, credits?: [{name, role, publishing_pct?, master_pct?}] }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** the created song.
post.summary: Create a song

get.description:
Runs the dedup heuristic (ISRC + title+artist fuzzy) and returns groups of suspected duplicates for human review.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `min_score` (0–1, default 0.85).
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ groups: [{score, reason, songs: [{id, title, artist, isrc}]}] }`.
get.summary: Find candidate duplicate songs in the org

post.description:
Picks `keep_song_id` as the survivor and folds metadata, credits, audio, and statement matches from `merge_song_ids` into it.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Body:** `{ keep_song_id: int, merge_song_ids: int[] }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ keep_song_id, merged_count, transferred: {credits, audio_assets, transactions} }`.
post.summary: Merge duplicate songs into a single canonical record

delete.description:
Permanently removes the song. Linked credits, splits, and contract assets are cleaned up.

**Path parameter:** `song_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the song's org.
**Response:** `{ success: true }`.

get.description:
Returns the full song record, including credits, splits, contracts, releases, and health score.

**Path parameter:** `song_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the song's org.
**Response:** `{ song: {...}, credits: [...], splits: [...], contracts: [...], releases: [...], audio_assets: [...], health: {...} }`.
get.summary: Get song detail

patch.description:
Patches song metadata (title, ISRC, ISWC, release_status, etc.). Edit history captures every field change.

**Path parameter:** `song_id`.
**Body:** any subset of writable song fields.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the song's org.
**Response:** the updated song.

get.description:
Returns the ordered set of checklist items the user must tick off for a song before it's ready (lyrics, splits, ISRC, audio, registration, etc.) plus their state.

**Path parameter:** `song_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the song's org.
**Response:** `{ items: [{key, label, complete, due_at, completed_at, completed_by}], pct_complete }`.
get.summary: Get a song's release-readiness checklist

patch.description:
Bulk-updates the supplied checklist items' completion state.

**Path parameter:** `song_id`.
**Body:** `{ items: [{key, complete: bool}] }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the song's org.
**Response:** the updated checklist (same shape as GET).

post.description:
Adds a creator credit (writer / producer / featured / etc.) with a publishing % and master % share. Recomputes the song's split totals.

**Path parameter:** `song_id`.
**Body:** `{ creator_id?: int, name?: string, role: string, publishing_pct?: number, master_pct?: number, notes?: string, ipi?: string, pro_society?: string }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the song's org.
**Response:** the created credit.
post.summary: Add a credit to a song

delete.description:
Removes a credit from the song. Splits totals are recomputed automatically.

**Path parameters:** `song_id`, `credit_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the song's org.
**Response:** `{ success: true }`.

patch.description:
Patches a credit's role, publishing %, master %, or notes.

**Path parameters:** `song_id`, `credit_id`.
**Body:** any subset of `{ role, publishing_pct, master_pct, notes }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the song's org.
**Response:** the updated credit.

post.description:
Used when a credit was scraped from DSPs and conflicts with the manual catalog. Lets the user accept, reject, or merge it.

**Path parameters:** `song_id`, `credit_id`.
**Body:** `{ resolution: "accept"|"reject"|"merge", merge_into_credit_id?: int }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the song's org.
**Response:** `{ song_id, credit_id, resolution, resulting_credit_id }`.
post.summary: Resolve a pending credit conflict

post.description:
Records a DSP id (and optional URL) on a Song so the platform can pull stream counts and credits from it later.

**Path parameter:** `song_id`.
**Body:** `{ dsp: "spotify"|"apple"|..., dsp_id, url? }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the song's org.
**Response:** `DSPLinkResponse` — the created link row.
post.summary: Attach a DSP link (Spotify, Apple, etc.) to a song

delete.description:
Removes a stored DSP id (Spotify/Apple/etc.) from a song. The DSP record itself is unaffected.

**Path parameters:** `song_id`, `link_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the song's org.
**Response:** `{ success: true }`.
delete.summary: Delete a DSP link from a song

post.description:
Creates a duplicate entry that links back to the original via `parent_song_id`. Useful for creating remix/edit variants from an existing master.

**Path parameter:** `song_id`.
**Body:** `{ title?: string }` to override the duplicated title.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the song's org.
**Response:** the new duplicated song.
post.summary: Duplicate a song

delete.description:
Detaches the song from its `group_id` so it stands alone again.

**Path parameter:** `song_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the song's org.
**Response:** `{ success: true }`.
delete.summary: Remove a song from its variant group

get.description:
Returns the full audit trail of field-level changes on a song with user attribution and timestamps.

**Path parameter:** `song_id`.
**Query:** `limit`, `offset`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the song's org.
**Response:** `{ total, history: [{id, field, old_value, new_value, changed_by, changed_at}] }`.
get.summary: Get song edit history

get.description:
Renders the audit trail of edits made to a song into a branded PDF — useful for compliance reviews.

**Path parameter:** `song_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the song's org.
**Response:** `application/pdf` download.
get.summary: Export a song's edit history as PDF

post.description:
Flips `release_status` to `RELEASED` and stamps the release date.

**Path parameter:** `song_id`.
**Body:** `{ release_date?: date }` — defaults to today.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the song's org.
**Response:** the updated song.
post.summary: Mark song as released

get.description:
Aggregates DSP stream counts, listener counts, and chart positions for a song across connected DSPs.

**Path parameter:** `song_id`.
**Query:** `dsp` (`spotify|apple|all`), `start_date`, `end_date`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the song's org.
**Response:** `{ totals: {streams, listeners}, by_dsp: [...], by_period: [{period, streams}] }`.
get.summary: Get a song's streaming/DSP performance summary

get.description:
Returns the most recent valuation calculated for the song. Returns 404 if no valuation has been computed yet.

**Path parameter:** `song_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the song's org.
**Response:** `{ song_id, valuation_cents, low_cents, high_cents, method, computed_at, parameters }`.

schema.title: Response Get Song Valuation Api Songs  Song Id  Valuation Get

get.summary: Get latest song valuation

post.description:
Runs the catalog valuation engine (industry multiples + Black Box Algorithm) for the song and persists the result.

**Path parameter:** `song_id`.
**Body:** `{ discount_rate?, multiple?, override_horizon_years?: int }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the song's org.
**Response:** the persisted valuation row (same shape as GET).
post.summary: Compute a new song valuation

get.description:
Returns every valuation ever computed for the song, newest first.

**Path parameter:** `song_id`.
**Query:** `limit`, `offset`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the song's org.
**Response:** `{ total, valuations: [{id, valuation_cents, low_cents, high_cents, method, computed_at, parameters}] }`.

schema.title: Response Get Song Valuation History Api Songs  Song Id  Valuations Get

get.summary: List historical song valuations

post.description:
Stores the Spotify track id on the Song so future scrapes/credit pulls know where to look.

**Body:** `{ song_id, spotify_id }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the song's org.
**Response:** `{ song_id, spotify_id, linked_at }`.
post.summary: Link a Spotify track to an existing Song

post.description:
Creates a Song for each selected playlist track (deduping by ISRC) and links to the supplied creator.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Body:** `{ playlist_url_or_id, creator_id?, selected_spotify_ids?: string[] }`. When `selected_spotify_ids` is omitted every track is imported.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ created, skipped, song_ids: [...] }`.
post.summary: Import tracks from a Spotify playlist into the catalog

post.description:
Resolves a Spotify playlist URL/ID and returns the tracklist with metadata so the user can confirm before import. Nothing is persisted.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Body:** `{ playlist_url_or_id: string }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ playlist_name, owner, track_count, tracks: [{spotify_id, title, artist, album, isrc, duration_ms}] }`.
post.summary: Preview a Spotify playlist before import

post.description:
Proxies a Spotify search query through the platform's API credentials so the UI can offer a search-first import flow.

**Body:** `{ q, type?: "track"|"album"|"artist" (default track), limit?: int }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT.
**Response:** `{ results: [{spotify_id, type, name, artist, isrc?, image_url}] }`.
post.summary: Search Spotify for tracks/artists/albums

post.description:
Broader counterpart of `/analyze-linked`: ignores Song/Work linkage and just queues an AudioAnalysis job for any AudioAsset in the org that doesn't have one yet (or whose previous analysis failed).

**Path parameter:** `org_id` — Organization ID.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** `{ message, queued }`.
post.summary: Queue audio analysis for every AudioAsset without one

post.description:
Walks the org's AudioAssets that are already linked to a Song/Work but have no AudioAnalysis row yet, and enqueues them for fingerprinting/key/BPM analysis.

**Path parameter:** `org_id` — Organization ID.
**Optional query:** `creator_id` (limit to one creator's catalog).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** `{ total_assets, queued }`.
post.summary: Queue audio analysis for every linked AudioAsset that lacks it

get.description:
Groups StorageScanResult rows by `scan_batch_id` and returns one row per batch with totals. Used to power the Scan History view.

**Path parameter:** `org_id` — Organization ID.
**Optional query:** `limit` (default 20).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** `List[{ scan_batch_id, started_at, link_id, creator_id, total, pending, approved, rejected }]`.
get.summary: List recent scan batches with summary counts

get.description:
Lists immediate folder/file children at `path` in the org's connected `provider` so the New Link wizard can render a folder picker. Read-only; no scanning is triggered.

**Path parameter:** `org_id` — Organization ID.
**Required query:** `provider` (e.g. `DROPBOX`).
**Optional query:** `path` (default root, provider-specific format), `container_name` (bucket / drive id when applicable).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** `{ provider, path, files: [{name, path, is_folder, size, modified}] }`.
get.summary: Browse a folder in a connected provider

post.description:
Auto-approves every PENDING StorageScanResult in `scan_batch_id` whose match confidence meets `min_confidence`. Equivalent to calling the per-result approve endpoint with the proposed match for each row, but in a single transaction.

**Path parameter:** `org_id` — Organization ID.
**Body (`BulkApproveRequest`):** `scan_batch_id` (from the batches endpoint), `min_confidence` (`HIGH` / `MEDIUM` / `LOW`, default `HIGH`).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** `{ approved, skipped, errors }`.
post.summary: Bulk-approve a scan batch's high-confidence matches

get.description:
Reports how much of the catalog has audio attached and how much of that audio has been analyzed. Powers the Coverage widget on the storage dashboard.

**Path parameter:** `org_id` — Organization ID.
**Optional query:** `creator_id` to scope to a single roster.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** `{ total_songs, songs_with_audio, songs_unlinked, songs_analyzed, songs_queued, songs_failed, total_assets, audio_coverage_pct, analysis_coverage_pct }`.
get.summary: Audio + analysis coverage stats for the org

get.description:
Returns every CreatorStorageLink in the org — a link is a (creator, provider, folder_path) tuple that storage scans crawl for new audio. Optionally filter to a single creator.

**Path parameter:** `org_id` — Cadence Organization ID.
**Optional query:** `creator_id` — restrict to a single Creator.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** `List[{ id, creator_id, provider, folder_path, container_name, scan_recursive, auto_scan_enabled, auto_scan_frequency, last_scanned_at }]`.
get.summary: List a creator's connected cloud-storage folders

post.description:
Pins a folder in the org's connected provider (Dropbox / Google Drive / OneDrive / S3) to a Creator. Future scans will index audio files under that path and propose Song matches. The folder must be reachable by the org's IntegrationAccount for `provider`.

**Path parameter:** `org_id` — Organization ID.
**Body (`CreatorStorageLinkCreate`):** `creator_id`, `provider` (default `DROPBOX`), `folder_path`, optional `container_name` (bucket / drive id), `scan_recursive` (default true), `auto_scan_enabled` (default false), `auto_scan_frequency` (`DAILY` / `WEEKLY` / `MONTHLY`).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** `{ id, creator_id, provider, folder_path }`.
post.summary: Connect a cloud-storage folder to a creator

delete.description:
Removes the CreatorStorageLink. Already-imported AudioAssets and match results are preserved; only the future-scan binding is broken.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `link_id`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** `{ message: "Storage link deleted" }`.

put.description:
Patches an existing CreatorStorageLink. Use the dedicated `/schedule` endpoint to change auto-scan timing without touching the path.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `link_id`.
**Body (`CreatorStorageLinkUpdate`):** any subset of `folder_path`, `container_name`, `scan_recursive`, `auto_scan_enabled`, `auto_scan_frequency`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** `{ message: "Storage link updated" }`.

Toggles whether the storage link is included in scheduled scans and at what cadence. The platform scheduler runs daily and picks up enabled links whose `auto_scan_frequency` window has elapsed.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `link_id`.
**Body:** `{ auto_scan_enabled: bool, auto_scan_frequency?: "DAILY" | "WEEKLY" | "MONTHLY" }`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** `{ message, auto_scan_enabled, auto_scan_frequency }`.
put.summary: Configure a link's automatic scan schedule

post.description:
Enqueues a background job that fans out scans across every CreatorStorageLink in the org and follows up with audio analysis for any new imports. Returns immediately with a `scan_batch_id` the UI can poll via `/batches`.

**Path parameter:** `org_id` — Organization ID.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** `{ scan_batch_id, queued: true }`.
post.summary: Trigger an asynchronous org-wide deep scan

get.description:
Returns one row per IntegrationAccount of provider type (`DROPBOX`, `GOOGLE_DRIVE`, `ONEDRIVE`, `S3`) — these are the providers the link/browse endpoints can target.

**Path parameter:** `org_id` — Organization ID.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** `List[{ provider, account_email, connected_at, status }]`.
get.summary: List the org's connected cloud-storage providers

get.description:
Returns the queue of StorageScanResult rows produced by recent scans — each row is a candidate Song/Work match for an imported AudioAsset that needs human review.

**Path parameter:** `org_id` — Organization ID.
**Optional query:** `creator_id`, `status` (`PENDING` / `APPROVED` / `REJECTED`), `confidence` (`HIGH` / `MEDIUM` / `LOW`), `scan_batch_id`, `limit`, `offset`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** `{ items: [...], total }` ordered by newest first.
get.summary: List storage-scan match results

post.description:
Accepts the proposed match (or a manual override) and binds the imported AudioAsset to the chosen Song/Work. Three modes:
1. Accept the proposed match — body `{}`.
2. Override with an existing record — body `{ song_id }` or `{ work_id }`.
3. Create a new Song on the fly — body `{ create_new: true, new_title, new_artist }`.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `result_id`.
**Body:** `ApproveRequest` (see modes above).

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** `{ message, song_id?, work_id?, asset_id }`.
post.summary: Approve a scan result and link the audio to a song / work

post.description:
Used to fix mistakes: moves the linked AudioAsset off its current Song/Work and onto a different one without going back through the approval queue.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `result_id`.
**Body (`ReassignMatchRequest`):** exactly one of `song_id` or `work_id`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** `{ message: "Match reassigned" }`.
post.summary: Reassign an already-approved scan result to a different song

post.description:
Marks a StorageScanResult as `REJECTED`. The underlying AudioAsset stays in the system but is excluded from the review queue. Re-scanning the folder will not re-propose this asset.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `result_id`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** `{ message: "Result rejected" }`.
post.summary: Reject a scan result

post.description:
Iterates every CreatorStorageLink in the org and runs the same import-and-match flow as the per-link scan endpoint. Long-running; consider using `/org-scan` (batched/async) for very large orgs.

**Path parameter:** `org_id` — Organization ID.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** `{ scan_batch_id, links_scanned, totals: { scanned, imported, matched, skipped, errors } }`.
post.summary: Run a fresh scan of every storage link in the org

post.description:
Kicks off a synchronous crawl of the linked folder. New audio files are imported into AudioAsset and run through the matcher to produce StorageScanResult rows the user can later approve or reject. Existing files are skipped via content hash.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `link_id`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be a member of the organization.

**Response:** `{ scan_batch_id, scanned, imported, matched, skipped, errors }`.
post.summary: Run a one-off scan against a single storage link

get.description:
Per-creator rollup: credit counts by DSP, by role (writer / producer / etc.), recently scraped, and pending claim issues.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `creator_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ creator_id, name, summary: {...}, pending: [...], last_scraped_at }`.
get.summary: Get a creator's streaming-credits detail summary

post.description:
Triggers an on-demand scrape against Spotify/Apple credit pages for the creator. Synchronous; returns when the scrape completes.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `creator_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ creator_id, fetched, added, updated, errors }`.
post.summary: Re-scrape DSP credits for a creator

delete.description:
Invalidates the existing token so the public page returns 410.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `creator_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ success: true }`.
delete.summary: Revoke the public share link for a creator's credits

post.description:
Creates (or rotates and replaces) the public share token used by `/credits/{share_token}` to expose the creator's credits page without auth. Returns the new URL.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `creator_id`.
**Body:** `{ expires_in_days?: int, rotate?: bool }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ share_token, share_url, expires_at }`.
post.summary: Mint or rotate a public share link for a creator's credits

get.description:
Per-song detail behind the creator overview — DSP, role, ISRC, credit URL, and whether it's matched to a Song in the catalog.

**Path parameters:** `org_id`, `creator_id`.
**Query:** `dsp`, `role`, `matched` (bool), `q`, `limit`, `offset`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ total, songs: [{credit_id, dsp, track_title, artist, isrc, role, credit_url, matched_song_id}] }`.
get.summary: List the songs a creator has streaming credits on

get.description:
Returns the dashboard for songwriter/producer credits scraped from DSPs (Spotify/Apple) — total credited tracks per creator and missing-credit counts.

**Path parameter:** `org_id`.
**Query:** `dsp` (`spotify|apple|all`), `start_date`, `end_date`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — caller must be a member of the org.
**Response:** `{ creators: [{creator_id, name, total_credits, by_dsp: {...}, missing_count}] }`.
get.summary: Get the org-wide streaming credits overview

get.description:
Streams the binary attachment file.

**Path parameter:** `attachment_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — must be the ticket creator or staff.
**Response:** the file with the appropriate `Content-Type`.
get.summary: Download a support-ticket attachment

get.description:
Returns every ticket the user has filed and its current status.

**Query:** `status`, `limit`, `offset`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT.
**Response:** `{ total, tickets: [{id, subject, status, severity, created_at, last_reply_at}] }`.
get.summary: List the calling user's support tickets

post.description:
Creates a SupportTicket on behalf of the calling user. Optionally attach a screenshot via the attachment fields. Notifies platform staff.

**Body:** `{ subject, body, severity?: "low"|"normal"|"high", attachment_storage_object_ids?: int[] }`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT.
**Response:** the created ticket.
post.summary: File a support ticket

get.description:
Returns the ticket header plus the full reply thread (visible fields only — admin-only notes are excluded).

**Path parameter:** `ticket_id`.
**Auth:** Bearer JWT — must be the ticket creator.
**Response:** `{ id, subject, body, status, severity, replies: [{id, author, body, created_at}], attachments: [...] }`.
get.summary: Get a single support ticket with its replies

get.description:
Returns the cosmetic + identity fields used to skin the artist portal and PDF/CSV exports for the caller's organization: name, display name, logo, colors, tagline, account type.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be OWNER, ADMIN, or super-admin.

**Response:** `{ id, name, display_name, account_type, logo_url, logo_orientation, primary_color, secondary_color, tagline, updated_at }`.
get.summary: Get the caller's organization branding

put.description:
Patches branding fields on the caller's organization. Use the logo upload endpoint to change the logo URL itself; this endpoint is for text/colour fields and pre-uploaded URLs.

**Body (`UpdateBrandingRequest`):** any subset of `display_name`, `logo_url`, `logo_orientation`, `primary_color` (hex), `secondary_color` (hex), `tagline`.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be OWNER, ADMIN, or super-admin.

**Response:** `{ message, branding: { ...same shape as GET } }`.

post.description:
Multipart upload of a PNG/JPEG/SVG logo. The file is persisted to the configured object store and the resulting public URL is written to the organization's `logo_url`. Replaces any previous logo.

**Body (multipart/form-data):** `file` — the image. Max size and allowed mime types follow the platform upload limits.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be OWNER, ADMIN, or super-admin.

**Response:** `{ message, logo_url }` (the new public URL).
post.summary: Upload the organization logo image

get.description:
Lightweight roster lookup used by the assign-creators picker. Returns every Creator visible to the caller's org as `{id, name}` tuples so the admin UI can build a multi-select.

**Auth:** Bearer JWT. Caller must be OWNER, ADMIN, or super-admin.

**Response:** `List[{ id, name }]` sorted by `name`.
get.summary: List creators in the caller's organization

get.description:
Returns every User attached to the caller's organization, with their OrganizationMember role, roster permission, linked Creator (if any) and the list of creators they've been individually granted access to. Drives the Team / Members tab of the Tenant Admin console.
